class DashboardController < ApplicationController
  def index
    @sandboxes = policy_scope(Sandbox).includes(:user, :routes).order(:name)
    @vnc_active_names = running_vnc_names
  end

  def card
    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).includes(:user, :routes).find(params[:id])
    authorize sandbox
    vnc_active = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(helpers.dom_id(sandbox), partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: sandbox, vnc_active: vnc_active })
  end

  def stats
    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize sandbox, :stats?

    if sandbox.status == "running" && sandbox.container_id.present?
      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
      raw = container.stats(stream: false)

      # Handle case where container is shutting down and returns nil/incomplete stats
      if raw.present?
        networks = raw["networks"] || {}
        net_rx = networks.values.sum { |n| n["rx_bytes"] || 0 }
        net_tx = networks.values.sum { |n| n["tx_bytes"] || 0 }

        blkio = raw.dig("blkio_stats", "io_service_bytes_recursive") || []
        disk_read = blkio.select { |e| e["op"]&.downcase == "read" }.sum { |e| e["value"] || 0 }
        disk_write = blkio.select { |e| e["op"]&.downcase == "write" }.sum { |e| e["value"] || 0 }

        @stats = {
          cpu_percent: calculate_cpu_percent(raw),
          memory_mb: (raw.dig("memory_stats", "usage") || 0) / 1_048_576.0,
          memory_limit_mb: (raw.dig("memory_stats", "limit") || 0) / 1_048_576.0,
          net_rx: net_rx,
          net_tx: net_tx,
          disk_read: disk_read,
          disk_write: disk_write,
          pids: raw.dig("pids_stats", "current") || 0
        }
      end
    end

    render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: @stats, sandbox: sandbox }
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: nil, sandbox: nil }
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: nil, sandbox: sandbox }
  end

  private

  def running_vnc_names
    Docker::Container.all.filter_map { |c|
      name = c.info.dig("Names")&.first&.delete_prefix("/")
      name.delete_prefix("sc-vnc-") if name&.start_with?("sc-vnc-")
    }.to_set
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    Set.new
  end

  def calculate_cpu_percent(stats)
    cpu_delta = stats.dig("cpu_stats", "cpu_usage", "total_usage").to_f -
                stats.dig("precpu_stats", "cpu_usage", "total_usage").to_f
    system_delta = stats.dig("cpu_stats", "system_cpu_usage").to_f -
                   stats.dig("precpu_stats", "system_cpu_usage").to_f
    num_cpus = stats.dig("cpu_stats", "online_cpus") || 1

    return 0.0 if system_delta.zero?
    ((cpu_delta / system_delta) * num_cpus * 100.0).round(1)
  end
end

Done. Here's what was added:

- **"browser" pill** (indigo, with monitor icon) appears in the sandbox card's pill row whenever an active noVNC container is detected for that sandbox
- **"Close" button** appears next to the "Browser" button when a session is active — sends `DELETE /sandboxes/:id/vnc` to tear it down
- **Dashboard index** does a single `Docker::Container.all` call and filters `sc-vnc-*` names into a Set — no per-sandbox Docker round trips
- **Turbo Stream card refresh** (`card` action) calls `VncManager#active?` for the individual sandbox so the status stays correct after partial re-renders

The user wants the noVNC client to autoconnect to the VNC server when it opens, rather than showing the "Verbinden" (Connect) button that requires manual clicking. 

Looking at the current setup:
- The VNC URL is `/vnc/43/vnc.html?path=vnc/43/websockify`
- The noVNC client shows a "Verbinden" (Connect) button

noVNC supports auto-connect via URL parameters. The `autoconnect=true` parameter tells noVNC to connect automatically without requiring the user to click the connect button.

Let me check the VncManager to see where the URL is constructed.

I just need to append `autoconnect=true` to the query string in both places. I need to add the `autoconnect=true` parameter to both places where the VNC URL is constructed—in the `VncManager` method that returns the URL and in the `VncController#wait` action that sets the instance variable the wait page uses for redirection.

class VncManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  NOVNC_IMAGE = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_NOVNC_IMAGE", "gotget/novnc:latest")
  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
  DYNAMIC_DIR = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")

  class Error < StandardError; end

  # Opens a web-based VNC session for the given sandbox.
  # Returns the URL path to the noVNC session.
  def open(sandbox:)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    user = sandbox.user
    container_name = vnc_container_name(sandbox)

    # Idempotent: if noVNC container already running, return URL
    if container_running?(container_name)
      return vnc_url(sandbox)
    end

    pull_image
    ensure_network
    connect_sandbox_to_network(sandbox)

    # Write Traefik config early so it has time to detect the new route
    write_traefik_config(sandbox)

    create_novnc_container(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)

    # Give Traefik time to detect the new route configuration
    sleep 2

    vnc_url(sandbox)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to open browser: #{e.message}"
  rescue SystemCallError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to open browser: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Closes the web VNC session for the given sandbox.
  def close(sandbox:)
    container_name = vnc_container_name(sandbox)

    # Stop and remove noVNC container
    begin
      container = Docker::Container.get(container_name)
      container.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
      container.delete(force: true)
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
      # Already gone
    end

    # Delete Traefik config
    delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.error("VncManager: close failed for #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
  end

  # Returns true if the noVNC container is running AND its HTTP server is ready.
  # The container_running? check alone is insufficient — the websockify process
  # may not have bound to port 6080 yet when Docker reports the container as running.
  def active?(sandbox:)
    container_name = vnc_container_name(sandbox)
    return false unless container_running?(container_name)

    TCPSocket.new(container_name, 6080).close
    true
  rescue Errno::ECONNREFUSED, Errno::ETIMEDOUT, SocketError
    false
  end

  # Removes orphaned noVNC containers whose sandbox no longer exists or is not running.
  def cleanup_orphaned
    Docker::Container.all(all: true).each do |container|
      name = container.info.dig("Names")&.first&.delete_prefix("/")
      next unless name&.start_with?("sc-vnc-")

      labels = container.info["Labels"] || {}
      sandbox_id = labels["sandcastle.sandbox_id"]&.to_i

      sandbox = sandbox_id ? Sandbox.find_by(id: sandbox_id) : nil
      should_remove = sandbox.nil? || sandbox.status != "running"

      if should_remove
        container.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
        container.delete(force: true)
        Rails.logger.info("VncManager: removed orphaned noVNC container #{name}")

        # Clean up Traefik config if we have a sandbox_id
        if sandbox_id
          config_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "vnc-#{sandbox_id}.yml")
          File.delete(config_path) if File.exist?(config_path)
        end
      end
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.error("VncManager: orphan cleanup failed: #{e.message}")
  end

  private

  def vnc_container_name(sandbox)
    "sc-vnc-#{sandbox.full_name}"
  end

  def vnc_url(sandbox)
    id = sandbox.id
    # Strip prefix is /vnc/{id}, so all requests under /vnc/{id}/* are forwarded
    # to the noVNC container with the prefix removed. This keeps noVNC's relative
    # asset paths (package.json, core/*.js) resolving correctly within the prefix.
    # The ?path= tells noVNC where to connect the WebSocket; Traefik strips
    # /vnc/{id} leaving /websockify which websockify handles.
    "/vnc/#{id}/vnc.html?path=vnc/#{id}/websockify"
  end

  def container_running?(name)
    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
    container.json.dig("State", "Running") == true
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    false
  end

  def pull_image
    Docker::Image.get(NOVNC_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => NOVNC_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{NOVNC_IMAGE} — check network connectivity"
  end

  def ensure_network
    Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Network.create(NETWORK_NAME, "Driver" => "bridge")
  end

  def connect_sandbox_to_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    networks = container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return if networks.key?(NETWORK_NAME)

    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    # Container no longer exists - sync job will fix the DB state
    raise Error, "Sandbox container not found. Please refresh and try again."
  end

  def create_novnc_container(sandbox:, user:)
    container_name = vnc_container_name(sandbox)

    # Remove any existing container with this name
    begin
      old = Docker::Container.get(container_name)
      old.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
      old.delete(force: true)
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
      # No existing container
    end

    host_config = {
      "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
      "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "no" },
      "Memory" => 128 * 1024 * 1024, # 128MB — noVNC static files + websockify proxy
      "NanoCpus" => 250_000_000 # 0.25 CPU
    }

    # Run the noVNC image using its built-in launch.sh, which starts both the
    # mini-webserver (noVNC HTML/JS) and websockify (WS→RFB proxy) on port 6080.
    # Traefik routes everything under /vnc/{id} to this container; after
    # stripPrefix the websockify path becomes /websockify as noVNC expects.
    container = Docker::Container.create(
      "name" => container_name,
      "Image" => NOVNC_IMAGE,
      "Cmd" => [ "--vnc", "#{sandbox.full_name}:5900", "--listen", "6080" ],
      "HostConfig" => host_config,
      "Labels" => {
        "sandcastle.sandbox_id" => sandbox.id.to_s,
        "sandcastle.role" => "novnc"
      }
    )

    container.start
    container
  end

  def write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    id = sandbox.id
    container_name = vnc_container_name(sandbox)
    sandbox_name   = sandbox.full_name

    base_rule = if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
      "HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(`/vnc/#{id}`)"
    else
      "Host(`#{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/vnc/#{id}`)"
    end

    # Single router: all traffic under /vnc/{id} goes to the noVNC sidecar.
    # The noVNC container (gotget/novnc launch.sh) serves static HTML/JS on /
    # and proxies WebSocket VNC frames via websockify on /websockify, both on
    # port 6080. stripPrefix removes /vnc/{id} before forwarding, so noVNC's
    # relative asset paths (package.json, core/*.js, /websockify) resolve correctly.
    config = {
      "http" => {
        "routers" => {
          "vnc-#{id}" => {
            "rule" => base_rule,
            "service" => "vnc-#{id}",
            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
            "tls" => tls_config,
            "middlewares" => [ "vnc-auth-#{id}", "vnc-stripprefix-#{id}" ],
            "priority" => 100
          }
        },
        "middlewares" => {
          "vnc-auth-#{id}" => {
            "forwardAuth" => {
              "address" => "http://sandcastle-web:80/vnc/auth",
              "trustForwardHeader" => true
            }
          },
          "vnc-stripprefix-#{id}" => {
            "stripPrefix" => {
              "prefixes" => [ "/vnc/#{id}" ]
            }
          }
        },
        "services" => {
          "vnc-#{id}" => {
            "loadBalancer" => {
              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{container_name}:6080" } ]
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

    File.write(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "vnc-#{id}.yml"), config.to_yaml)
  end

  def tls_config
    if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
      {}
    else
      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
    end
  end

  def delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
    path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "vnc-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  end
end

class VncController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    VncManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to vnc_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    id = @sandbox.id
    @vnc_url = vnc_redirect_url("/vnc/#{id}/vnc.html?path=vnc/#{id}/websockify")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    ready = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Browser session closed"
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full VNC URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def vnc_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL"] || ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/vnc/(\d+)})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original VNC URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

content.content:
title:	feat(snapshots): implement full composite snapshot management
state:	OPEN
author:	thieso2
labels:	
assignees:	
reviewers:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	38
url:	https://github.com/thieso2/Sandcastle/pull/38
additions:	1277
deletions:	125
auto-merge:	disabled
--
Adds a unified snapshot system that combines Docker container images with optional BTRFS copy-on-write snapshots of home and data directories.

Key changes:
- DB: new `snapshots` table with per-layer fields (docker_image, home_snapshot, data_snapshot) and size tracking
- Model: Snapshot AR model with layers() and total_size() helpers
- Policy: SnapshotPolicy (Pundit) for owner/admin authorization
- BtrfsHelper: snapshot_subvolume, delete_snapshot, subvolume_size, restore_subvolume methods for read-only BTRFS snapshot lifecycle
- SandboxManager: create_snapshot (composite), list_snapshots (DB + legacy import), destroy_snapshot (all layers), restore (per-layer support), import_legacy_snapshots (idempotent Docker→DB migration)
- API: POST /api/snapshots (create), GET/DELETE /api/snapshots/:name (show/destroy) with layers and data_subdir params; sandbox restore endpoint supports layers
- Web: sandbox show page with snapshot list + create modal; global /snapshots page; navbar link; sandbox create form uses richer snapshot labels
- CLI: `sandcastle snapshot create/list/show/destroy/restore` with --label, --layers, --data-subdir, --restore-layers flags; `sandbox create --from-snapshot`
- Installer: ensures /data/snapshots directory is created and owned
- Guide: updated snapshot CLI documentation with full examples

Backward compatible: existing Docker-only snapshots are auto-imported as DB records; legacy `snapshot` API method preserved as alias.

package api

import "time"

type Sandbox struct {
	ID               int       `json:"id"`
	Name             string    `json:"name"`
	FullName         string    `json:"full_name"`
	Status           string    `json:"status"`
	Image            string    `json:"image"`
	SSHPort          int       `json:"ssh_port"`
	PersistentVolume bool      `json:"persistent_volume"`
	MountHome        bool      `json:"mount_home"`
	DataPath         string    `json:"data_path,omitempty"`
	Temporary        bool      `json:"temporary"`
	Tailscale        bool           `json:"tailscale"`
	TailscaleIP      string         `json:"tailscale_ip,omitempty"`
	VNCEnabled       bool           `json:"vnc_enabled"`
	VNCGeometry      string         `json:"vnc_geometry,omitempty"`
	VNCDepth         int            `json:"vnc_depth,omitempty"`
	Routes           []SandboxRoute `json:"routes"`
	ConnectCommand   string         `json:"connect_command"`
	CreatedAt        time.Time `json:"created_at"`
}

type SandboxRoute struct {
	ID     int    `json:"id"`
	Domain string `json:"domain"`
	Port   int    `json:"port"`
	URL    string `json:"url"`
}

type ConnectInfo struct {
	Host        string `json:"host"`
	Port        int    `json:"port"`
	User        string `json:"user"`
	Command     string `json:"command"`
	TailscaleIP string `json:"tailscale_ip,omitempty"`
}

type User struct {
	ID           int       `json:"id"`
	Name         string    `json:"name"`
	EmailAddress string    `json:"email_address"`
	Admin        bool      `json:"admin"`
	Status       string    `json:"status"`
	SandboxCount int       `json:"sandbox_count"`
	CreatedAt    time.Time `json:"created_at"`
}

type Token struct {
	ID         int        `json:"id"`
	Name       string     `json:"name"`
	Prefix     string     `json:"prefix"`
	MaskedToken string    `json:"masked_token"`
	RawToken   string     `json:"raw_token,omitempty"`
	LastUsedAt *time.Time `json:"last_used_at"`
	ExpiresAt  *time.Time `json:"expires_at"`
	CreatedAt  time.Time  `json:"created_at"`
}

type SystemStatus struct {
	Docker    map[string]any   `json:"docker"`
	Sandboxes map[string]int   `json:"sandboxes"`
	Resources []map[string]any `json:"resources"`
}

type ServerInfo struct {
	Version   string           `json:"version"`
	Rails     string           `json:"rails"`
	Ruby      string           `json:"ruby"`
	Host      ServerHostInfo   `json:"host"`
	Sandboxes map[string]int   `json:"sandboxes"`
	Docker    ServerDockerInfo `json:"docker"`
	Users     ServerUserCounts `json:"users"`
}

type ServerHostInfo struct {
	Memory    HostMemory `json:"memory"`
	Disk      HostDisk   `json:"disk"`
	Load      HostLoad   `json:"load"`
	CPUCount  int        `json:"cpu_count"`
	Uptime    string     `json:"uptime"`
	Processes int        `json:"processes"`
}

type HostMemory struct {
	TotalGB     float64 `json:"total_gb"`
	UsedGB      float64 `json:"used_gb"`
	AvailableGB float64 `json:"available_gb"`
	Percent     float64 `json:"percent"`
}

type HostDisk struct {
	TotalGB     float64 `json:"total_gb"`
	UsedGB      float64 `json:"used_gb"`
	AvailableGB float64 `json:"available_gb"`
	Percent     float64 `json:"percent"`
}

type HostLoad struct {
	One     float64 `json:"one"`
	Five    float64 `json:"five"`
	Fifteen float64 `json:"fifteen"`
}

type ServerDockerInfo struct {
	Version           string   `json:"version"`
	Containers        int      `json:"containers"`
	ContainersRunning int      `json:"containers_running"`
	Images            int      `json:"images"`
	Runtimes          []string `json:"runtimes"`
}

type ServerUserCounts struct {
	Total  int `json:"total"`
	Admins int `json:"admins"`
}

type Snapshot struct {
	Name          string    `json:"name"`
	Label         string    `json:"label,omitempty"`
	SourceSandbox string    `json:"source_sandbox,omitempty"`
	Layers        []string  `json:"layers,omitempty"`
	// Legacy field for backward compat
	Image         string    `json:"image,omitempty"`
	DockerImage   string    `json:"docker_image,omitempty"`
	DockerSize    int64     `json:"docker_size,omitempty"`
	HomeSize      int64     `json:"home_size,omitempty"`
	DataSize      int64     `json:"data_size,omitempty"`
	TotalSize     int64     `json:"total_size,omitempty"`
	// Legacy size field
	Size          int64     `json:"size,omitempty"`
	// Legacy sandbox field
	Sandbox       string    `json:"sandbox,omitempty"`
	CreatedAt     time.Time `json:"created_at"`
}

type SnapshotRequest struct {
	Name      string   `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Label     string   `json:"label,omitempty"`
	Layers    []string `json:"layers,omitempty"`
	DataSubdir string  `json:"data_subdir,omitempty"`
}

type CreateSnapshotRequest struct {
	SandboxID  int      `json:"sandbox_id"`
	Name       string   `json:"name"`
	Label      string   `json:"label,omitempty"`
	Layers     []string `json:"layers,omitempty"`
	DataSubdir string   `json:"data_subdir,omitempty"`
}

type RestoreRequest struct {
	Snapshot string   `json:"snapshot"`
	Layers   []string `json:"layers,omitempty"`
}

type RouteRequest struct {
	Domain string `json:"domain"`
	Port   int    `json:"port,omitempty"`
}

type RouteResponse struct {
	ID          int    `json:"id"`
	SandboxID   int    `json:"sandbox_id"`
	SandboxName string `json:"sandbox_name"`
	Domain      string `json:"domain"`
	Port        int    `json:"port"`
	URL         string `json:"url"`
}

type CreateSandboxRequest struct {
<<<<<<< HEAD
	Name          string   `json:"name"`
	Image         string   `json:"image,omitempty"`
	Persistent    bool     `json:"persistent,omitempty"`
	Snapshot      string   `json:"snapshot,omitempty"`
	FromSnapshot  string   `json:"from_snapshot,omitempty"`
	RestoreLayers []string `json:"restore_layers,omitempty"`
	Tailscale     bool     `json:"tailscale,omitempty"`
	MountHome     bool     `json:"mount_home,omitempty"`
	DataPath      string   `json:"data_path,omitempty"`
	Temporary     bool     `json:"temporary,omitempty"`
=======
	Name        string `json:"name"`
	Image       string `json:"image,omitempty"`
	Persistent  bool   `json:"persistent,omitempty"`
	Snapshot    string `json:"snapshot,omitempty"`
	Tailscale   bool   `json:"tailscale,omitempty"`
	MountHome   bool   `json:"mount_home,omitempty"`
	DataPath    string `json:"data_path,omitempty"`
	Temporary   bool   `json:"temporary,omitempty"`
	VNCEnabled  bool   `json:"vnc_enabled"`
	VNCGeometry string `json:"vnc_geometry,omitempty"`
	VNCDepth    int    `json:"vnc_depth,omitempty"`
>>>>>>> main
}

type UpdateSandboxRequest struct {
	Temporary *bool `json:"temporary,omitempty"`
}

type CreateTokenRequest struct {
	EmailAddress string `json:"email_address"`
	Password     string `json:"password"`
	Name         string `json:"name"`
}

type CreateUserRequest struct {
	Name                 string `json:"name"`
	EmailAddress         string `json:"email_address"`
	Password             string `json:"password"`
	PasswordConfirmation string `json:"password_confirmation"`
	SSHPublicKey         string `json:"ssh_public_key,omitempty"`
	Admin                bool   `json:"admin,omitempty"`
}

type TailscaleEnableRequest struct {
	AuthKey string `json:"auth_key"`
}

type TailscaleLoginResponse struct {
	LoginURL string `json:"login_url"`
}

type TailscaleLoginStatus struct {
	Status      string `json:"status"`
	TailscaleIP string `json:"tailscale_ip,omitempty"`
	Hostname    string `json:"hostname,omitempty"`
	Tailnet     string `json:"tailnet,omitempty"`
	Error       string `json:"error,omitempty"`
}

type TailscaleStatus struct {
	Running            bool                `json:"running"`
	ContainerID        string              `json:"container_id"`
	Network            string              `json:"network"`
	ConnectedSandboxes int                 `json:"connected_sandboxes"`
	TailscaleIP        string              `json:"tailscale_ip"`
	Hostname           string              `json:"hostname"`
	Tailnet            string              `json:"tailnet"`
	Online             bool                `json:"online"`
	Sandboxes          []TailscaleSandbox  `json:"sandboxes"`
}

type TailscaleSandbox struct {
	Name string `json:"name"`
	IP   string `json:"ip"`
}

type APIError struct {
	Error string `json:"error"`
}

// Device auth types

type DeviceCodeRequest struct {
	ClientName string `json:"client_name"`
}

type DeviceCodeResponse struct {
	DeviceCode      string `json:"device_code"`
	UserCode        string `json:"user_code"`
	VerificationURL string `json:"verification_url"`
	ExpiresIn       int    `json:"expires_in"`
	Interval        int    `json:"interval"`
}

type DeviceTokenRequest struct {
	DeviceCode string `json:"device_code"`
}

type DeviceTokenResponse struct {
	Token string `json:"token"`
	Error string `json:"error,omitempty"`
}

content.content:
1→class SandboxesController < ApplicationController
     2→  before_action :set_sandbox, only: [ :show, :destroy, :start, :stop, :retry ]
     3→
     4→  def new
     5→    authorize Sandbox
     6→    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
     7→    @btrfs_available = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
     8→  end
     9→
    10→  def show
    11→    @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    12→                           .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
    13→    @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    14→  end
    15→
    16→  def create
    17→    authorize Sandbox
    18→
    19→    from_snapshot_name = params[:snapshot].presence
    20→
    21→    image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
    22→      snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: Current.user, name: from_snapshot_name)
    23→      snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{Current.user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    24→    else
    25→      params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    26→    end
    27→
    28→    # Build sandbox record
    29→    # Note: temporary sandboxes can only be created via CLI
    30→    sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.build(
    31→      name: params.require(:name),
    32→      status: "pending",
    33→      image: image,
    34→      persistent_volume: params[:persistent] == "1",
    35→      mount_home: params[:mount_home] == "1",
    36→      data_path: params[:data_path].presence,
    37→      tailscale: params[:tailscale] == "1",
    38→      vnc_enabled: params[:vnc_enabled] != "0",
    39→      vnc_geometry: Sandbox::VNC_GEOMETRIES.include?(params[:vnc_geometry]) ? params[:vnc_geometry] : "1280x900",
    40→      vnc_depth: Sandbox::VNC_DEPTHS.include?(params[:vnc_depth].to_i) ? params[:vnc_depth].to_i : 24,
    41→      temporary: false
    42→    )
    43→
    44→    if sandbox.persistent_volume
    45→      sandbox.volume_path = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    46→    end
    47→
    48→    if sandbox.save
    49→      # Enqueue async job
    50→      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
    51→
    52→      # Optimistic redirect - dashboard will update via Turbo
    53→      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Creating sandcastle #{sandbox.name}..."
    54→    else
    55→      @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    56→      flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
    57→      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    58→    end
    59→  rescue => e
    60→    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    61→    flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{e.message}"
    62→    render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    63→  end
    64→
    65→  def destroy
    66→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
    67→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
    68→      return
    69→    end
    70→
    71→    @sandbox.start_job("destroying")
    72→    SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
    73→
    74→    respond_to do |format|
    75→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Destroying sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
    76→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    77→    end
    78→  end
    79→
    80→  def start
    81→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
    82→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
    83→      return
    84→    end
    85→
    86→    @sandbox.start_job("starting")
    87→    SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
    88→
    89→    respond_to do |format|
    90→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Starting sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
    91→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    92→    end
    93→  end
    94→
    95→  def stop
    96→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
    97→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
    98→      return
    99→    end
   100→
   101→    @sandbox.start_job("stopping")
   102→    SandboxStopJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   103→
   104→    respond_to do |format|
   105→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Stopping sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
   106→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
   107→    end
   108→  end
   109→
   110→  def retry
   111→    return unless @sandbox.job_failed?
   112→
   113→    @sandbox.update!(job_error: nil)
   114→
   115→    case @sandbox.status
   116→    when "destroyed", "pending"
   117→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Cannot retry creation. Please create a new sandbox."
   118→    when "stopped"
   119→      SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   120→      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Retrying start..."
   121→    when "running"
   122→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Sandbox is already running"
   123→    end
   124→  end
   125→
   126→  private
   127→
   128→  def set_sandbox
   129→    @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
   130→    authorize @sandbox
   131→  end
   132→end
   133→

content.content:
1→class SnapshotsController < ApplicationController
     2→  before_action :set_snapshot, only: %i[destroy clone]
     3→
     4→  def index
     5→    authorize Snapshot
     6→    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
     7→  end
     8→
     9→  # POST /sandboxes/:id/snapshot
    10→  def create_for_sandbox
    11→    # Member route provides sandbox id as params[:id]
    12→    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    13→    authorize sandbox, :snapshot?
    14→
    15→    layers = params[:layers].present? ? Array(params[:layers]) : nil
    16→
    17→    SandboxManager.new.create_snapshot(
    18→      sandbox: sandbox,
    19→      name: params.require(:name),
    20→      label: params[:label].presence,
    21→      layers: layers,
    22→      data_subdir: params[:data_subdir].presence
    23→    )
    24→
    25→    redirect_to sandbox_path(sandbox), notice: "Snapshot created."
    26→  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    27→    redirect_back_or_to root_path, alert: "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
    28→  end
    29→
    30→  def destroy
    31→    SandboxManager.new.destroy_snapshot(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    32→    redirect_to snapshots_path, notice: "Snapshot deleted."
    33→  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    34→    redirect_to snapshots_path, alert: "Failed to delete snapshot: #{e.message}"
    35→  end
    36→
    37→  # POST /snapshots/:name/clone — redirect to new sandbox form pre-filled with snapshot
    38→  def clone
    39→    authorize @snapshot, :show?
    40→    redirect_to new_sandbox_path(snapshot: @snapshot.name)
    41→  end
    42→
    43→  private
    44→
    45→  def set_snapshot
    46→    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    47→    authorize @snapshot
    48→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    49→    SandboxManager.new.import_legacy_snapshots(Current.user)
    50→    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    51→    authorize @snapshot
    52→  end
    53→end
    54→

1→class Snapshot < ApplicationRecord
     2→  belongs_to :user
     3→
     4→  validates :name, presence: true,
     5→    uniqueness: { scope: :user_id },
     6→    format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric, hyphens, or underscores" }
     7→
     8→  scope :for_user, ->(user) { where(user: user) }
     9→
    10→  def layers
    11→    layers = []
    12→    layers << "container" if docker_image.present?
    13→    layers << "home" if home_snapshot.present?
    14→    layers << "data" if data_snapshot.present?
    15→    layers
    16→  end
    17→
    18→  def total_size
    19→    (docker_size || 0) + (home_size || 0) + (data_size || 0)
    20→  end
    21→end
    22→

1→Rails.application.routes.draw do
     2→  root "dashboard#index"
     3→
     4→  # Mount Action Cable for WebSocket connections
     5→  mount ActionCable.server => "/cable"
     6→
     7→  resource :session
     8→  resources :passwords, param: :token
     9→  resource :invite, only: [ :edit, :update ], path_names: { edit: "" }

1→class SandboxManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  DEFAULT_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest"
     4→
     5→  class Error < StandardError; end
     6→
     7→  def create(user:, name:, image: DEFAULT_IMAGE, persistent: false, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)
     8→    # Build sandbox record (not saved yet)
     9→    sandbox = user.sandboxes.build(
    10→      name: name,
    11→      image: image,
    12→      status: "pending",
    13→      persistent_volume: persistent,
    14→      mount_home: mount_home,
    15→      data_path: data_path,
    16→      temporary: temporary
    17→    )
    18→
    19→    if persistent
    20→      sandbox.volume_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    21→    end
    22→
    23→    # Validate before doing expensive operations
    24→    sandbox.validate!
    25→
    26→    # Create directories FIRST (can fail fast before saving record)
    27→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    28→
    29→    # Now safe to save
    30→    sandbox.save!
    31→
    32→    # Pull image
    33→    ensure_image(image)
    34→
    35→    # Create and start container
    36→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
    37→
    38→    # Connect to Tailscale if requested
    39→    if (tailscale || user.tailscale_auto_connect?) && user.tailscale_enabled?
    40→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    41→    end
    42→
    43→    sandbox
    44→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    45→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    46→    raise Error, "Failed to create container: #{e.message}"
    47→  rescue => e
    48→    # If anything fails before save, no DB record is created
    49→    # If anything fails after save, mark as destroyed
    50→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    51→    raise Error, e.message
    52→  end
    53→
    54→  # Public method for job usage
    55→  def create_container_and_start(sandbox:, user:)
    56→    container = Docker::Container.create(
    57→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
    58→      "Image" => sandbox.image,
    59→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
    60→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
    61→      "HostConfig" => {
    62→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
    63→        "PortBindings" => {
    64→          "22/tcp" => [ { "HostPort" => sandbox.ssh_port.to_s } ]
    65→        },
    66→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
    67→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
    68→      }
    69→    )
    70→
    71→    container.start
    72→    container.refresh!
    73→    raise Error, "Container failed to start (state: #{container.json.dig("State", "Status")})" unless container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    74→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running")
    75→  end
    76→
    77→  # Public method for job usage
    78→  def ensure_image(image)
    79→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
    80→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    81→    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
    82→  end
    83→
    84→  # Public method for job usage
    85→  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    86→    # Directories bind-mounted into Sysbox containers must be world-writable
    87→    # because Sysbox maps container root to a high host UID (via /etc/subuid)
    88→    # that won't match the directory owner.
    89→
    90→    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
    91→    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)
    92→
    93→    if sandbox.mount_home
    94→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
    95→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
    96→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, dir)
    97→    end
    98→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
    99→      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
   100→      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)
   101→
   102→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   103→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
   104→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, dir)
   105→    end
   106→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   107→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(sandbox.volume_path)
   108→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, sandbox.volume_path)
   109→    end
   110→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
   111→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   112→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   113→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
   114→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, dir)
   115→    end
   116→  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
   117→    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
   118→  end
   119→
   120→  def destroy(sandbox:, keep_volume: false)
   121→    begin
   122→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   123→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   124→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   125→    end
   126→
   127→    begin
   128→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   129→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   130→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   131→    end
   132→
   133→    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   134→
   135→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   136→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   137→    end
   138→
   139→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   140→      begin
   141→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   142→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   143→        container.delete(force: true)
   144→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   145→        # Container already gone
   146→      end
   147→    end
   148→
   149→    unless keep_volume
   150→      FileUtils.rm_rf(sandbox.volume_path) if sandbox.volume_path.present?
   151→    end
   152→
   153→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   154→  end
   155→
   156→  def start(sandbox:)
   157→    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
   158→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"
   159→
   160→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   161→    container.start
   162→    sandbox.update!(status: "running")
   163→
   164→    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   165→
   166→    sandbox
   167→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   168→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   169→    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
   170→  end
   171→
   172→  def stop(sandbox:)
   173→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"
   174→
   175→    begin
   176→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   177→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   178→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   179→    end
   180→
   181→    RouteManager.new.suspend_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   182→
   183→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   184→    container.stop(t: 10)
   185→    sandbox.update!(status: "stopped")
   186→    sandbox
   187→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   188→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   189→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   190→  end
   191→
   192→  def status(sandbox:)
   193→    return { state: "destroyed" } if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   194→
   195→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   196→    info = container.json
   197→    {
   198→      state: info.dig("State", "Status"),
   199→      running: info.dig("State", "Running"),
   200→      started_at: info.dig("State", "StartedAt"),
   201→      pid: info.dig("State", "Pid")
   202→    }
   203→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   204→    { state: "not_found" }
   205→  end
   206→
   207→  # Create a composite snapshot (Docker image + optional BTRFS layers).
   208→  # Returns a Snapshot ActiveRecord object.
   209→  #
   210→  # layers: array of "container", "home", "data", "workspace"
   211→  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
   212→  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
   213→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   214→
   215→    user = sandbox.user
   216→    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
   217→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data workspace]
   218→
   219→    snap = Snapshot.new(
   220→      user: user,
   221→      name: name,
   222→      label: label,
   223→      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
   224→      data_subdir: data_subdir
   225→    )
   226→
   227→    # ── Container layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   228→    if requested_layers.include?("container")
   229→      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   230→      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   231→      image = container.commit(
   232→        repo: repo,
   233→        tag: name,
   234→        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
   235→      )
   236→      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
   237→      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
   238→    end
   239→
   240→    # ── Home layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   241→    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   242→      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   243→      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
   244→      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
   245→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
   246→        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
   247→        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
   248→      end
   249→    end
   250→
   251→    # ── Data layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   252→    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   253→      if data_subdir.present?
   254→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   255→      else
   256→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   257→      end
   258→      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
   259→      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
   260→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
   261→        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
   262→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
   263→      end
   264→    end
   265→
   266→    # ── Workspace layer (BTRFS only) ─────────────────────────────────────────
   267→    if requested_layers.include?("workspace") && sandbox.persistent_volume? && sandbox.volume_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   268→      workspace_src  = sandbox.volume_path
   269→      workspace_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/workspace"
   270→      if Dir.exist?(workspace_src)
   271→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(workspace_src, workspace_dest)
   272→        # Store workspace alongside data_snapshot when no data layer taken
   273→        snap.data_snapshot ||= workspace_dest
   274→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(workspace_dest)
   275→      end
   276→    end
   277→
   278→    snap.save!
   279→    snap
   280→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   281→    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
   282→  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   283→    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
   284→  end
   285→
   286→  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
   287→  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
   288→    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
   289→    {
   290→      name: snap.name,
   291→      image: snap.docker_image,
   292→      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   293→      created_at: snap.created_at
   294→    }
   295→  end
   296→
   297→  def list_snapshots(user:)
   298→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   299→    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
   300→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   301→    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
   302→  end
   303→
   304→  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
   305→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   306→    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
   307→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   308→    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   309→  end
   310→
   311→  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
   312→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)
   313→
   314→    if snap
   315→      # Remove Docker image
   316→      if snap.docker_image.present?
   317→        begin
   318→          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
   319→        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   320→          # Already gone
   321→        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   322→          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
   323→        end
   324→      end
   325→
   326→      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
   327→      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
   328→        begin
   329→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)
   330→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   331→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete home snapshot #{snap.home_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   332→        end
   333→      end
   334→
   335→      if snap.data_snapshot.present?
   336→        begin
   337→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.data_snapshot)
   338→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   339→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete data snapshot #{snap.data_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   340→        end
   341→      end
   342→
   343→      snap.destroy!
   344→    else
   345→      # Legacy: try to find and remove Docker image directly
   346→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{name}"
   347→      begin
   348→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref).remove
   349→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   350→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   351→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   352→        raise Error, "Failed to destroy snapshot: #{e.message}"
   353→      end
   354→    end
   355→  end
   356→
   357→  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
   358→    user = sandbox.user
   359→    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?
   360→
   361→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
   362→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)
   363→
   364→    # Determine the Docker image to use
   365→    if snap&.docker_image.present?
   366→      image_ref = snap.docker_image
   367→    else
   368→      # Fall back to legacy naming
   369→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
   370→    end
   371→
   372→    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
   373→    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
   374→    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))
   375→
   376→    # Validate the Docker image exists if we need it
   377→    if restore_container
   378→      begin
   379→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   380→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   381→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{snapshot_name}' not found"
   382→      end
   383→    end
   384→
   385→    begin
   386→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   387→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   388→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   389→    end
   390→
   391→    begin
   392→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   393→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   394→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   395→    end
   396→
   397→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   398→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   399→    end
   400→
   401→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   402→      begin
   403→        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   404→        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   405→        old_container.delete(force: true)
   406→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   407→        # Already gone
   408→      end
   409→    end
   410→
   411→    # ── Restore home directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   412→    if restore_home
   413→      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   414→      begin
   415→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
   416→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   417→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
   418→      end
   419→    end
   420→
   421→    # ── Restore data directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   422→    if restore_data
   423→      if snap.data_subdir.present?
   424→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   425→      else
   426→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   427→      end
   428→      begin
   429→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
   430→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   431→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
   432→      end
   433→    end
   434→
   435→    # ── Recreate container ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   436→    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image
   437→
   438→    container = Docker::Container.create(
   439→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
   440→      "Image" => final_image,
   441→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
   442→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
   443→      "HostConfig" => {
   444→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
   445→        "PortBindings" => {
   446→          "22/tcp" => [ { "HostPort" => sandbox.ssh_port.to_s } ]
   447→        },
   448→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
   449→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
   450→      }
   451→    )
   452→
   453→    container.start
   454→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, image: final_image, status: "running")
   455→
   456→    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
   457→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   458→    end
   459→
   460→    sandbox
   461→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   462→    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
   463→  end
   464→
   465→  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
   466→  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   467→    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   468→
   469→    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
   470→      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
   471→      repo_tags.each do |tag|
   472→        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
   473→        next unless repo == repo_prefix
   474→        next if tag_name.blank?
   475→        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)
   476→
   477→        Snapshot.create!(
   478→          user: user,
   479→          name: tag_name,
   480→          docker_image: tag,
   481→          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
   482→          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
   483→          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
   484→        )
   485→      end
   486→    end
   487→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
   488→    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
   489→  end
   490→
   491→  def snapshot_json(snap)
   492→    {
   493→      name: snap.name,
   494→      label: snap.label,
   495→      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   496→      layers: snap.layers,
   497→      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
   498→      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
   499→      home_size: snap.home_size,
   500→      data_size: snap.data_size,
   501→      total_size: snap.total_size,
   502→      created_at: snap.created_at
   503→    }
   504→  end
   505→
   506→  def connect_info(sandbox:)
   507→    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
   508→    user = sandbox.user.name
   509→    info = {
   510→      host: host,
   511→      port: sandbox.ssh_port,
   512→      user: user,
   513→      command: sandbox.connect_command(host: host)
   514→    }
   515→
   516→    # If Tailscale is enabled, wait for and use the bridge network IP for direct SSH access
   517→    # This works if the client is on the same Tailscale network (routes advertised by sidecar)
   518→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   519→      ts_ip = wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   520→      if ts_ip.present?
   521→        info[:host] = ts_ip
   522→        info[:port] = 22
   523→        info[:command] = "ssh #{user}@#{ts_ip}"
   524→        info[:tailscale_ip] = ts_ip
   525→      end
   526→    end
   527→
   528→    info
   529→  end
   530→
   531→  private
   532→
   533→  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
   534→    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
   535→    max_attempts.times do
   536→      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status
   537→
   538→      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
   539→      if sandbox.status != "running"
   540→        sleep delay
   541→        next
   542→      end
   543→
   544→      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
   545→      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   546→      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?
   547→
   548→      sleep delay
   549→    end
   550→    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
   551→    nil
   552→  end
   553→
   554→  def container_env(user, sandbox)
   555→    env = [
   556→      "SANDCASTLE_USER=#{user.name}",
   557→      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.ssh_public_key}"
   558→    ]
   559→    env << "USER_EMAIL=#{user.email_address}" if user.email_address.present?
   560→    env << "USER_FULLNAME=#{user.full_name}" if user.full_name.present?
   561→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED=#{sandbox.vnc_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   562→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY=#{sandbox.vnc_geometry}"
   563→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH=#{sandbox.vnc_depth}"
   564→    env
   565→  end
   566→
   567→  def container_runtime
   568→    @container_runtime ||= begin
   569→      runtimes = Docker.info["Runtimes"] || {}
   570→      if runtimes.key?("sysbox-runc")
   571→        "sysbox-runc"
   572→      else
   573→        Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: sysbox-runc not available, falling back to runc (Docker-in-Docker will not work inside sandboxes)")
   574→        "runc"
   575→      end
   576→    end
   577→  end
   578→
   579→  def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
   580→    binds = []
   581→    if sandbox.mount_home
   582→      binds << "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home:/home/#{user.name}"
   583→    end
   584→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   585→      binds << "#{sandbox.volume_path}:/workspace"
   586→    end
   587→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   588→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   589→      binds << "#{host_path}:/data"
   590→    end
   591→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount separately if not mounting full home
   592→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   593→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   594→      binds << "#{host_path}:/home/#{user.name}/.config/google-chrome"
   595→    end
   596→    binds
   597→  end
   598→end
   599→

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully read all the requested files. Here's a summary of what I found:

## File Contents Summary

### 1. **app/views/shared/_navbar.html.erb** (55 lines)
Navigation bar with logo, links to Dashboard, Snapshots, Guide, Tailscale, Admin (if admin), and Jobs. Also displays user name, settings link, and logout button.

### 2. **app/views/sandboxes/show.html.erb** (159 lines)
Sandbox detail page showing:
- Sandbox name and status badge
- SSH port information
- Snapshots tab with a table listing all snapshots for the current sandbox
- Create Snapshot modal with:
  - Snapshot name field (required, with pattern validation)
  - Optional description/label
  - Layer selection checkboxes (container, home, data, workspace)
  - BTRFS availability indicator
- Clone and Delete buttons for each snapshot

### 3. **app/views/sandboxes/new.html.erb** (190 lines)
Create sandbox form with sections for:
- Basic Information (name, image)
- Restore from Snapshot (if snapshots exist)
- Storage Options (persistent volume, mount home, data path)
- Network Options (Tailscale, if user has it enabled)
- VNC/Display Options (enable/disable, resolution, color depth)
- JavaScript for auto-generating sandbox names and toggling VNC options

### 4. **app/controllers/sandboxes_controller.rb** (132 lines)
Sandbox controller with actions:
- `new` - lists available snapshots and checks BTRFS availability
- `show` - filters snapshots by source sandbox
- `create` - creates sandbox from form params (with snapshot support)
- `destroy` - enqueues destruction job
- `start` - enqueues start job
- `stop` - enqueues stop job
- `retry` - retries failed operations

### 5. **app/controllers/snapshots_controller.rb** (53 lines)
Snapshots controller with actions:
- `index` - lists user snapshots
- `create_for_sandbox` - creates snapshot for a specific sandbox
- `destroy` - deletes snapshot
- `clone` - redirects to new sandbox form with snapshot pre-filled
- Legacy snapshot import on `set_snapshot` if not found in DB

### 6. **app/models/snapshot.rb** (21 lines)
Snapshot ActiveRecord model with:
- Belongs to User
- Name validation (lowercase alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores)
- `layers` method - returns array of available layers (container, home, data)
- `total_size` - sums docker_size, home_size, data_size

### 7. **config/routes.rb** (119 lines)
Routes configuration with:
- Sandboxes resource with member routes: start, stop, retry, stats, card, terminal (open/wait/status/close), vnc (open/wait/status/close), snapshot (create)
- Snapshots resource with index/destroy and clone member route
- API namespace with snapshots CRUD, sandbox restore, snapshot restore
- Tailscale routes
- Admin namespace with dashboard, users, sandboxes management, jobs

### 8. **app/services/sandbox_manager.rb** (598 lines)
Core sandbox management service with snapshot-related methods:

**Snapshot Methods:**
- **`create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label:, layers:, data_subdir:)`** - Creates composite snapshots with:
  - Container layer (docker commit)
  - Home layer (BTRFS snapshot)
  - Data layer (BTRFS snapshot)
  - Workspace layer (BTRFS snapshot)
  - Saves Snapshot DB record with all metadata

- **`list_snapshots(user:)`** - Lists all user snapshots (imports legacy ones first)

- **`destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)`** - Deletes snapshot:
  - Removes Docker image
  - Removes BTRFS snapshots
  - Destroys Snapshot record

- **`restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers:)`** - Restores snapshot:
  - Cleans up terminal/VNC/Tailscale
  - Stops/removes old container
  - Restores home directory (BTRFS)
  - Restores data directory (BTRFS)
  - Recreates container with snapshot image
  - Reconnects Tailscale if applicable

**Helper Methods:**
- `snapshot_json` - Serializes Snapshot record to JSON
- `import_legacy_snapshots` - Imports legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records

All files are properly structured and use Rails conventions with Tailwind CSS for styling.

<% content_for(:title) { "#{@sandbox.name} — Sandcastle" } %>

<%= @sandbox.name %>

<%= @sandbox.status %> Port <%= @sandbox.ssh_port %>

<%= link_to "Back to Dashboard", root_path, class: "text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-700 text-sm" %>
<%# ── Snapshots tab ── %>

Snapshots

<% if @sandbox_snapshots.empty? %>
No snapshots yet. Create one to save the current state of this sandbox.
<% else %> <% @sandbox_snapshots.each do |snap| %> <% end %>

class SnapshotsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_snapshot, only: %i[destroy clone]

  def index
    authorize Snapshot
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
  end

  # POST /sandboxes/:id/snapshot
  def create_for_sandbox
    # Member route provides sandbox id as params[:id]
    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize sandbox, :snapshot?

    layers = params[:layers].present? ? Array(params[:layers]) : nil

    SandboxManager.new.create_snapshot(
      sandbox: sandbox,
      name: params.require(:name),
      label: params[:label].presence,
      layers: layers,
      data_subdir: params[:data_subdir].presence
    )

    redirect_to sandbox_path(sandbox), notice: "Snapshot created."
  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    redirect_back_or_to root_path, alert: "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
  end

  def destroy
    SandboxManager.new.destroy_snapshot(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    redirect_to snapshots_path, notice: "Snapshot deleted."
  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    redirect_to snapshots_path, alert: "Failed to delete snapshot: #{e.message}"
  end

  # POST /snapshots/:name/clone — redirect to new sandbox form pre-filled with snapshot
  def clone
    authorize @snapshot, :show?
    redirect_to new_sandbox_path(snapshot: @snapshot.name)
  end

  private

  def set_snapshot
    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    authorize @snapshot
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    SandboxManager.new.import_legacy_snapshots(Current.user)
    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    authorize @snapshot
  end
end

1→class SandboxesController < ApplicationController
     2→  before_action :set_sandbox, only: [ :show, :destroy, :start, :stop, :retry ]
     3→
     4→  def new
     5→    authorize Sandbox
     6→    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
     7→    @btrfs_available = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
     8→  end
     9→
    10→  def show
    11→    @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    12→                           .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
    13→    @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    14→  end
    15→
    16→  def create
    17→    authorize Sandbox
    18→
    19→    from_snapshot_name = params[:snapshot].presence
    20→
    21→    image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
    22→      snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: Current.user, name: from_snapshot_name)
    23→      snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{Current.user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    24→    else
    25→      params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    26→    end
    27→
    28→    # Build sandbox record
    29→    # Note: temporary sandboxes can only be created via CLI
    30→    sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.build(
    31→      name: params.require(:name),
    32→      status: "pending",
    33→      image: image,
    34→      persistent_volume: params[:persistent] == "1",
    35→      mount_home: params[:mount_home] == "1",
    36→      data_path: params[:data_path].presence,
    37→      tailscale: params[:tailscale] == "1",
    38→      vnc_enabled: params[:vnc_enabled] != "0",
    39→      vnc_geometry: Sandbox::VNC_GEOMETRIES.include?(params[:vnc_geometry]) ? params[:vnc_geometry] : "1280x900",
    40→      vnc_depth: Sandbox::VNC_DEPTHS.include?(params[:vnc_depth].to_i) ? params[:vnc_depth].to_i : 24,
    41→      temporary: false
    42→    )
    43→
    44→    if sandbox.persistent_volume
    45→      sandbox.volume_path = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    46→    end
    47→
    48→    if sandbox.save
    49→      # Enqueue async job
    50→      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
    51→
    52→      # Optimistic redirect - dashboard will update via Turbo
    53→      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Creating sandcastle #{sandbox.name}..."
    54→    else
    55→      @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    56→      flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
    57→      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    58→    end
    59→  rescue => e
    60→    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    61→    flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{e.message}"
    62→    render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    63→  end
    64→
    65→  def destroy
    66→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
    67→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
    68→      return
    69→    end
    70→
    71→    @sandbox.start_job("destroying")
    72→    SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
    73→
    74→    respond_to do |format|
    75→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Destroying sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
    76→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    77→    end
    78→  end
    79→
    80→  def start
    81→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
    82→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
    83→      return
    84→    end
    85→
    86→    @sandbox.start_job("starting")
    87→    SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
    88→
    89→    respond_to do |format|
    90→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Starting sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
    91→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    92→    end
    93→  end
    94→
    95→  def stop
    96→    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
    97→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
    98→      return
    99→    end
   100→
   101→    @sandbox.start_job("stopping")
   102→    SandboxStopJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
   103→
   104→    respond_to do |format|
   105→      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Stopping sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
   106→      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
   107→    end
   108→  end
   109→
   110→  def retry
   111→    return unless @sandbox.job_failed?
   112→
   113→    @sandbox.update!(job_error: nil)
   114→
   115→    case @sandbox.status
   116→    when "destroyed", "pending"
   117→      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Cannot retry creation. Please create a new sandbox."

class SandboxesController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_sandbox, only: [ :show, :destroy, :start, :stop, :retry ]

  def new
    authorize Sandbox
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    @btrfs_available = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
  end

  def show
    @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
                           .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
    @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
  end

  def create
    authorize Sandbox

    from_snapshot_name = params[:snapshot].presence

    image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
      snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: Current.user, name: from_snapshot_name)
      snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{Current.user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    else
      params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    end

    # Build sandbox record
    # Note: temporary sandboxes can only be created via CLI
    sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.build(
      name: params.require(:name),
      status: "pending",
      image: image,
      persistent_volume: params[:persistent] == "1",
      mount_home: params[:mount_home] == "1",
      data_path: params[:data_path].presence,
      tailscale: params[:tailscale] == "1",
      vnc_enabled: params[:vnc_enabled] != "0",
      vnc_geometry: Sandbox::VNC_GEOMETRIES.include?(params[:vnc_geometry]) ? params[:vnc_geometry] : "1280x900",
      vnc_depth: Sandbox::VNC_DEPTHS.include?(params[:vnc_depth].to_i) ? params[:vnc_depth].to_i : 24,
      temporary: false
    )

    if sandbox.persistent_volume
      sandbox.volume_path = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    end

    if sandbox.save
      # Enqueue async job
      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)

      # Optimistic redirect - dashboard will update via Turbo
      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Creating sandcastle #{sandbox.name}..."
    else
      @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
      flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  rescue => e
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{e.message}"
    render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
  end

  def destroy
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("destroying")
    SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Destroying sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def start
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("starting")
    SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Starting sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def stop
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("stopping")
    SandboxStopJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Stopping sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def retry
    return unless @sandbox.job_failed?

    @sandbox.update!(job_error: nil)

    case @sandbox.status
    when "destroyed", "pending"
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Cannot retry creation. Please create a new sandbox."
    when "stopped"
      SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Retrying start..."
    when "running"
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Sandbox is already running"
    end
  end

  private

  def set_sandbox
    @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize @sandbox
  end
end

data-controller="sandbox-polling" data-sandbox-polling-url-value="<%= card_sandbox_path(sandbox) %>" <% end %>> <%# Line 1: Status, name, pills %>
<%# Status indicator with animation %> <%= link_to sandbox.name, sandbox_path(sandbox), class: "font-mono font-medium text-gray-900 hover:text-blue-600 transition-colors" %> <%# Job status badge %> <% if sandbox.job_in_progress? %> <%= sandbox.job_status.humanize %> <% elsif sandbox.job_failed? %> Failed <% end %> <%# Existing feature pills (temp, home, data, volume, Tailscale, routes) %> <% if sandbox.temp? %> TEMP <% end %> <% if sandbox.mount_home? %> home <% end %> <% if sandbox.data_path.present? %> data<%= ":#{sandbox.data_path}" unless sandbox.data_path == "." %> <% end %> <% if sandbox.persistent_volume? %> volume <% end %> <% if sandbox.tailscale? %> Tailscale <% end %> <% if local_assigns[:vnc_active] %> browser <% end %> <% sandbox.routes.each do |route| %> <%= route.domain %>:<%= route.port %> <% end %>
<%# Action buttons %>
<% if sandbox.job_in_progress? %> Processing... <% elsif sandbox.job_failed? %> <%= button_to retry_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Retry <% end %> <% else %> <% if sandbox.status == "stopped" %> <%= button_to "Start", start_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-green-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-green-700 transition-colors" %> <% elsif sandbox.status == "running" %> <%= button_to terminal_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "text-xs px-2 py-1.5 bg-gray-700 text-white rounded hover:bg-gray-800 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Terminal <% end %> <%= button_to vnc_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "text-xs px-2 py-1.5 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Browser <% end %> <% if local_assigns[:vnc_active] %> <%= button_to vnc_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :delete, class: "text-xs px-2 py-1.5 bg-indigo-100 text-indigo-700 rounded hover:bg-indigo-200 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Close <% end %> <% end %> <%= button_to "Stop", stop_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-yellow-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-yellow-700 transition-colors" %> <% end %>
<%= button_to "Destroy", sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :delete, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-red-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-red-700 transition-colors", data: { action: "click->inline-confirm#confirm", confirm_message: "Destroy?" } %>
<% end %>
<%# Error display %> <% if sandbox.job_failed? %>
Operation Failed

<%= sandbox.job_error %>

<% end %> <%# Container info and stats %>
<% if sandbox.container_id.present? %> <%= sandbox.container_id.first(12) %> · <% end %> <%= sandbox.image.sub("ghcr.io/thieso2/", "") %> · Created <%= time_ago_in_words(sandbox.created_at) %> ago <% if sandbox.status == "running" %> · Running for <%= time_ago_in_words(sandbox.updated_at) %> <% elsif sandbox.status == "stopped" %> · Stopped <%= time_ago_in_words(sandbox.updated_at) %> ago <% end %>
<% if sandbox.status == "running" %> <%= turbo_frame_tag "sandbox_stats_#{sandbox.id}", src: stats_sandbox_path(sandbox), loading: :lazy do %> loading stats... <% end %> <% end %>
