content.text: Now rewrite the sandbox show page to be a full detail page with actions + snapshots:

input.content:
<% content_for(:title) { "#{@sandbox.name} — Sandcastle" } %>
<%# Header %>

<%= @sandbox.name %>

<%= @sandbox.status %>

<%= @sandbox.image.sub("ghcr.io/thieso2/", "") %> · SSH port <%= @sandbox.ssh_port %> · Created <%= time_ago_in_words(@sandbox.created_at) %> ago

<%= link_to root_path, class: "text-sm text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-700 shrink-0" do %> ← Dashboard <% end %>
<%# Actions %>
<% if @sandbox.status == "running" %> <%= button_to terminal_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-gray-700 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-gray-800 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1.5" do %> Terminal <% end %> <%= button_to vnc_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-blue-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1.5" do %> Browser <% end %> <%= button_to "Stop", stop_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-yellow-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-yellow-700 transition-colors" %> <% elsif @sandbox.status == "stopped" %> <%= button_to "Start", start_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-green-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-green-700 transition-colors" %> <% end %>
<%= button_to "Destroy", sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :delete, data: { confirm: "Destroy #{@sandbox.name}? This cannot be undone." }, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-red-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-red-700 transition-colors" %>
<%# Snapshots %>

Snapshots

<% if @sandbox_snapshots.empty? %>
No snapshots yet. Create one to save the current state of this sandbox.
<% else %> <% @sandbox_snapshots.each do |snap| %> <% end %>
Name Layers Size Created
<%= snap[:name] %> <% if snap[:label].present? %> <%= snap[:label] %> <% end %> <% (snap[:layers] || []).each do |layer| %> <%= layer[0].upcase %> <% end %> <%= number_to_human_size(snap[:total_size] || 0) %> <%= snap[:created_at]&.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") %>
<%= link_to "New sandbox", new_sandbox_path(snapshot: snap[:name]), class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800 text-xs" %> <%= button_to "Delete", snapshot_path(snap[:name]), method: :delete, data: { confirm: "Delete snapshot \"#{snap[:name]}\"?" }, class: "text-red-600 hover:text-red-800 text-xs bg-transparent border-0 cursor-pointer p-0" %>
<% end %>
<%# Create Snapshot Modal %>

Create Snapshot

<%= form_with url: sandbox_snapshot_path(@sandbox), method: :post, class: "px-6 py-4 space-y-4" do |f| %>
<%= f.label :name, "Snapshot Name", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= f.text_field :name, required: true, placeholder: Date.today.iso8601, pattern: "[a-z][a-z0-9_\\-]*", class: "w-full px-3 py-2 border border-gray-300 rounded-lg focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-blue-500 text-sm" %>
<%= f.label :label, "Description (optional)", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= f.text_field :label, placeholder: "e.g. before migration", class: "w-full px-3 py-2 border border-gray-300 rounded-lg focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-blue-500 text-sm" %>

Layers to include

<% if @sandbox.mount_home && @btrfs %> <% end %> <% if @sandbox.data_path.present? && @btrfs %> <% end %> <% if @sandbox.persistent_volume && @btrfs %> <% end %> <% unless @btrfs %>

BTRFS not detected — only the container layer is available.

<% end %>
<%= f.submit "Create Snapshot", class: "px-4 py-2 bg-blue-600 text-white text-sm font-medium rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700 cursor-pointer" %>
<% end %>

toolUseResult.originalFile:
class SnapshotsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_snapshot, only: %i[destroy clone]

  def index
    authorize Snapshot
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
  end

  # POST /sandboxes/:id/snapshot
  def create_for_sandbox
    # Member route provides sandbox id as params[:id]
    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize sandbox, :snapshot?

    layers = params[:layers].present? ? Array(params[:layers]) : nil

    SandboxManager.new.create_snapshot(
      sandbox: sandbox,
      name: params.require(:name),
      label: params[:label].presence,
      layers: layers,
      data_subdir: params[:data_subdir].presence
    )

    redirect_to sandbox_path(sandbox), notice: "Snapshot created."
  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    redirect_back_or_to root_path, alert: "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
  end

  def destroy
    SandboxManager.new.destroy_snapshot(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    redirect_to snapshots_path, notice: "Snapshot deleted."
  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    redirect_to snapshots_path, alert: "Failed to delete snapshot: #{e.message}"
  end

  # POST /snapshots/:name/clone — redirect to new sandbox form pre-filled with snapshot
  def clone
    authorize @snapshot, :show?
    redirect_to new_sandbox_path(snapshot: @snapshot.name)
  end

  private

  def set_snapshot
    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    authorize @snapshot
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    SandboxManager.new.import_legacy_snapshots(Current.user)
    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    authorize @snapshot
  end
end

content.text:
Done. Here's what changed:

**Dashboard card:**
- Sandbox name is now a clickable link → detail page
- Camera "Snapshot" button (running sandboxes) opens a lightweight modal right on the card (name + description, uses default layers)

**Sandbox detail page (`/sandboxes/:id`):**
- Action bar: Terminal, Browser, Snapshot, Stop/Start, Destroy
- Snapshot list with "New sandbox" (pre-fills the create form) and Delete per row
- Full create snapshot modal with layer selection (container/home/data/workspace depending on BTRFS)

**Sandbox create form:**
- Snapshot dropdown now shows `name — description [layers] (from sandbox)` for richer context

**Removed:**
- "Snapshots" link from the navbar
- `SnapshotsController#destroy` no longer redirects to the gone snapshots page — uses `redirect_back_or_to root_path` instead

<% content_for(:title) { "#{@sandbox.name} — Sandcastle" } %>
<%# Header %>

<%= @sandbox.name %>

<%= @sandbox.status %>

<%= @sandbox.image.sub("ghcr.io/thieso2/", "") %> · SSH port <%= @sandbox.ssh_port %> · Created <%= time_ago_in_words(@sandbox.created_at) %> ago

<%= link_to root_path, class: "text-sm text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-700 shrink-0" do %> ← Dashboard <% end %>
<%# Actions %>
<% if @sandbox.status == "running" %> <%= button_to terminal_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-gray-700 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-gray-800 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1.5" do %> Terminal <% end %> <%= button_to vnc_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-blue-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1.5" do %> Browser <% end %> <%= button_to "Stop", stop_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-yellow-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-yellow-700 transition-colors" %> <% elsif @sandbox.status == "stopped" %> <%= button_to "Start", start_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-green-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-green-700 transition-colors" %> <% end %>
<%= button_to "Destroy", sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :delete, data: { confirm: "Destroy #{@sandbox.name}? This cannot be undone." }, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-red-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-red-700 transition-colors" %>
<%# Snapshots %>

Snapshots

<% if @sandbox_snapshots.empty? %>
No snapshots yet. Create one to save the current state of this sandbox.
<% else %> <% @sandbox_snapshots.each do |snap| %> <% end %>
Name Layers Size Created
<%= snap[:name] %> <% if snap[:label].present? %> <%= snap[:label] %> <% end %> <% (snap[:layers] || []).each do |layer| %> <%= layer[0].upcase %> <% end %> <%= number_to_human_size(snap[:total_size] || 0) %> <%= snap[:created_at]&.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") %>
<%= link_to "New sandbox", new_sandbox_path(snapshot: snap[:name]), class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800 text-xs" %> <%= button_to "Delete", snapshot_path(snap[:name]), method: :delete, data: { confirm: "Delete snapshot \"#{snap[:name]}\"?" }, class: "text-red-600 hover:text-red-800 text-xs bg-transparent border-0 cursor-pointer p-0" %>
<% end %>
<%# Create Snapshot Modal %>

Create Snapshot

<%= form_with url: sandbox_snapshot_path(@sandbox), method: :post, class: "px-6 py-4 space-y-4" do |f| %>
<%= f.label :name, "Snapshot Name", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= f.text_field :name, required: true, placeholder: Date.today.iso8601, pattern: "[a-z][a-z0-9_\\-]*", class: "w-full px-3 py-2 border border-gray-300 rounded-lg focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-blue-500 text-sm" %>
<%= f.label :label, "Description (optional)", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= f.text_field :label, placeholder: "e.g. before migration", class: "w-full px-3 py-2 border border-gray-300 rounded-lg focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-blue-500 text-sm" %>

Layers to include

<% if @sandbox.mount_home && @btrfs %> <% end %> <% if @sandbox.data_path.present? && @btrfs %> <% end %> <% if @sandbox.persistent_volume && @btrfs %> <% end %> <% unless @btrfs %>

BTRFS not detected — only the container layer is available.

<% end %>
<%= f.submit "Create Snapshot", class: "px-4 py-2 bg-blue-600 text-white text-sm font-medium rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700 cursor-pointer" %>
<% end %>

The user wants:
1. Just a name field (no description/label field) in the dashboard modal
2. Default should be a timestamp - but the pattern `[a-z][a-z0-9_\-]*` doesn't allow dates like `2026-02-01` because they start with a digit. 

The fix:
1. Change the default placeholder/value to use a format like `snap-20260219` (starts with a letter)
2. Remove the description/label field from the dashboard modal (keep it simple - just name)
3. Keep the pattern validation or adjust it

For the dashboard modal, the user wants just a name field with a sensible timestamp default, so I'll remove the description field and set the JavaScript default to the `snap-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS` format. - Or change the pattern to also allow digits at start

I'll use `snap-YYYYMMDD` as the default, set via JS. And remove the description field from both modals. content.text: Two issues: the date `2026-02-01` fails the `[a-z]` pattern (starts with digit), and the modal has an extra description field. Fix: use a `snap-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS` default via JS, remove the label field from both modals.

<%# Status indicator with animation %> <%= link_to sandbox.name, sandbox_path(sandbox), class: "font-mono font-medium text-gray-900 hover:text-blue-600 transition-colors" %> <%# Job status badge %> <% if sandbox.job_in_progress? %> <%= sandbox.job_status.humanize %> <% elsif sandbox.job_failed? %> Failed <% end %> <%# Existing feature pills (temp, home, data, volume, Tailscale, routes) %> <% if sandbox.temp? %> TEMP <% end %> <% if sandbox.mount_home? %> home <% end %> <% if sandbox.data_path.present? %> data<%= ":#{sandbox.data_path}" unless sandbox.data_path == "." %> <% end %> <% if sandbox.persistent_volume? %> volume <% end %> <% if sandbox.tailscale? %> Tailscale <% end %> <% if local_assigns[:vnc_active] %> browser <% end %> <% sandbox.routes.each do |route| %> <%= route.domain %>:<%= route.port %> <% end %>
<%# Action buttons %>
<% if sandbox.job_in_progress? %> Processing... <% elsif sandbox.job_failed? %> <%= button_to retry_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Retry <% end %> <% else %> <% if sandbox.status == "stopped" %> <%= button_to "Start", start_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-green-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-green-700 transition-colors" %> <% elsif sandbox.status == "running" %> <%= button_to terminal_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "text-xs px-2 py-1.5 bg-gray-700 text-white rounded hover:bg-gray-800 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Terminal <% end %> <%= button_to vnc_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "text-xs px-2 py-1.5 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Browser <% end %> <% if local_assigns[:vnc_active] %> <%= button_to vnc_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :delete, class: "text-xs px-2 py-1.5 bg-indigo-100 text-indigo-700 rounded hover:bg-indigo-200 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Close <% end %> <% end %> <%= button_to "Stop", stop_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-yellow-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-yellow-700 transition-colors" %> <% end %>
<%= button_to "Destroy", sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :delete, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-red-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-red-700 transition-colors", data: { action: "click->inline-confirm#confirm", confirm_message: "Destroy?" } %>
<% end %>
<%# Error display %> <% if sandbox.job_failed? %>
Operation Failed

<% end %> <%# Container info and stats %>
<% if sandbox.container_id.present? %> <%= sandbox.container_id.first(12) %> · <% end %> <%= sandbox.image.sub("ghcr.io/thieso2/", "") %> · Created <%= time_ago_in_words(sandbox.created_at) %> ago <% if sandbox.status == "running" %> · Running for <%= time_ago_in_words(sandbox.updated_at) %> <% elsif sandbox.status == "stopped" %> · Stopped <%= time_ago_in_words(sandbox.updated_at) %> ago <% end %>
<% if sandbox.status == "running" %> <%= turbo_frame_tag "sandbox_stats_#{sandbox.id}", src: stats_sandbox_path(sandbox), loading: :lazy do %> loading stats... <% end %> <% end %>

<% if sandbox.job_in_progress? %> Processing... <% elsif sandbox.job_failed? %> <%= button_to retry_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Retry <% end %> <% else %> <% if sandbox.status == "stopped" %> <%= button_to "Start", start_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-green-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-green-700 transition-colors" %> <% elsif sandbox.status == "running" %> <%= button_to terminal_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "text-xs px-2 py-1.5 bg-gray-700 text-white rounded hover:bg-gray-800 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Terminal <% end %> <%= button_to vnc_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "text-xs px-2 py-1.5 bg-blue-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Browser <% end %> <% if local_assigns[:vnc_active] %> <%= button_to vnc_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :delete, class: "text-xs px-2 py-1.5 bg-indigo-100 text-indigo-700 rounded hover:bg-indigo-200 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Close <% end %> <% end %> <%= button_to "Stop", stop_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-yellow-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-yellow-700 transition-colors" %> <% end %>
<%= button_to "Destroy", sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :delete, class: "text-xs px-3 py-1.5 bg-red-600 text-white rounded hover:bg-red-700 transition-colors", data: { action: "click->inline-confirm#confirm", confirm_message: "Destroy?" } %>
<% end %>
<%# Error display %> <% if sandbox.job_failed? %>
Operation Failed

<%= sandbox.job_error %>

<% end %> <%# Container info and stats %>
<% if sandbox.container_id.present? %> <%= sandbox.container_id.first(12) %> · <% end %> <%= sandbox.image.sub("ghcr.io/thieso2/", "") %> · Created <%= time_ago_in_words(sandbox.created_at) %> ago <% if sandbox.status == "running" %> · Running for <%= time_ago_in_words(sandbox.updated_at) %> <% elsif sandbox.status == "stopped" %> · Stopped <%= time_ago_in_words(sandbox.updated_at) %> ago <% end %>
<% if sandbox.status == "running" %> <%= turbo_frame_tag "sandbox_stats_#{sandbox.id}", src: stats_sandbox_path(sandbox), loading: :lazy do %> loading stats... <% end %> <% end %>
<%# Per-card snapshot modal %>

Snapshot — <%= sandbox.name %>

<%= form_with url: snapshot_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :post, class: "px-5 py-4 space-y-3" do |f| %> <%= f.text_field :name, required: true, id: "snap-name-#{sandbox.id}", pattern: "[a-z][a-z0-9_\\-]*", class: "w-full px-3 py-2 border border-gray-300 rounded-lg focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-blue-500 text-sm font-mono" %>
<%= f.submit "Create Snapshot", class: "px-4 py-1.5 bg-blue-600 text-white text-sm font-medium rounded-lg hover:bg-blue-700 cursor-pointer" %>
<% end %>

<% content_for(:title) { "#{@sandbox.name} — Sandcastle" } %>
<%# Header %>

<%= @sandbox.name %>

<%= @sandbox.status %>

<%= @sandbox.image.sub("ghcr.io/thieso2/", "") %> · SSH port <%= @sandbox.ssh_port %> · Created <%= time_ago_in_words(@sandbox.created_at) %> ago

<%= link_to root_path, class: "text-sm text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-700 shrink-0" do %> ← Dashboard <% end %>
<%# Actions %>
<% if @sandbox.status == "running" %> <%= button_to terminal_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-gray-700 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-gray-800 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1.5" do %> Terminal <% end %> <%= button_to vnc_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, form: { target: "_blank" }, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-blue-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1.5" do %> Browser <% end %> <%= button_to "Stop", stop_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-yellow-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-yellow-700 transition-colors" %> <% elsif @sandbox.status == "stopped" %> <%= button_to "Start", start_sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :post, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-green-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-green-700 transition-colors" %> <% end %>
<%= button_to "Destroy", sandbox_path(@sandbox), method: :delete, data: { confirm: "Destroy #{@sandbox.name}? This cannot be undone." }, class: "px-3 py-1.5 bg-red-600 text-white text-sm rounded hover:bg-red-700 transition-colors" %>
<%# Snapshots %>

Snapshots

<% if @sandbox_snapshots.empty? %>
No snapshots yet. Create one to save the current state of this sandbox.
<% else %> <% @sandbox_snapshots.each do |snap| %> <% end %>
Name Layers Size Created
<%= snap[:name] %> <% if snap[:label].present? %> <%= snap[:label] %> <% end %> <% (snap[:layers] || []).each do |layer| %> <%= layer[0].upcase %> <% end %> <%= number_to_human_size(snap[:total_size] || 0) %> <%= snap[:created_at]&.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") %>
<%= link_to "New sandbox", new_sandbox_path(snapshot: snap[:name]), class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800 text-xs" %> <%= button_to "Delete", snapshot_path(snap[:name]), method: :delete, data: { confirm: "Delete snapshot \"#{snap[:name]}\"?" }, class: "text-red-600 hover:text-red-800 text-xs bg-transparent border-0 cursor-pointer p-0" %>
<% end %>

Create Snapshot

<%= f.label :name, "Snapshot Name", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= f.text_field :name, required: true, placeholder: Date.today.iso8601, pattern: "[a-z][a-z0-9_\\-]*", class: "w-full px-3 py-2 border border-gray-300 rounded-lg focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-blue-500 text-sm" %>
<%= f.label :label, "Description (optional)", class: "block text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-1" %> <%= f.text_field :label, placeholder: "e.g. before migration", class: "w-full px-3 py-2 border border-gray-300 rounded-lg focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:border-blue-500 text-sm" %>

Layers to include

content.text:
Done. Now when the modal opens:
- The name field is pre-filled with `snap-20260219-143022` (current timestamp, starts with a letter so it passes validation)
- The field is auto-selected so you can just type to override it
- The description field is gone — just a name and submit

content.content:
diff --git a/images/sandbox/Dockerfile b/images/sandbox/Dockerfile
index deafb6f..fb74849 100644
--- a/images/sandbox/Dockerfile
+++ b/images/sandbox/Dockerfile
@@ -27,8 +27,17 @@ RUN if [ "$TARGETARCH" = "amd64" ]; then \
       && rm google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
       && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*; \
     else \
-      apt-get update \
+      # Ubuntu ships chromium as a snap-only wrapper since 19.10 — unusable in containers.
+      # Use xtradeb/apps PPA which provides a real .deb. Force noble suite since
+      # xtradeb may not yet have packages for the current Ubuntu release (25.10/plucky).
+      apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends software-properties-common \
+      && add-apt-repository -y ppa:xtradeb/apps \
+      && CODENAME=$(lsb_release -cs) \
+      && find /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ -name "xtradeb*" -exec \
+           sed -i "s/\b$CODENAME\b/noble/g" {} \; \
+      && apt-get update \
       && apt-get install -y chromium \
+      && apt-get purge -y --auto-remove software-properties-common \
       && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*; \
     fi

vnc autoconnect does not work!

content.thinking:
Looking at the URL in the screenshot: `localhost:8443/vnc/77/vnc.html?path=vnc/77/websockify&autoconnect=true`

The problem is clear — the URL contains `&` (HTML entity) instead of `&`. This is Rails HTML-escaping the `&` in the URL when it's used in HTML attributes. The `autoconnect=true` parameter is being HTML-escaped.

So the URL is being rendered as `&autoconnect=true` instead of `&autoconnect=true`, which means noVNC doesn't see the `autoconnect` parameter at all.


The issue is that when these URL strings are rendered in HTML attributes, Rails automatically escapes the ampersand. I need to find where the URLs are being constructed in the controller and view, then either use `html_safe` to mark them as safe HTML or restructure how they're being passed to prevent the escaping. it gets rendered in the ERB template — the `&` is being HTML-escaped to `&`.

Looking at the wait template:
```erb
var vncUrl = "<%= j @vnc_url %>";
...
window.location.href = vncUrl;
```

The `j` helper is `escape_javascript`, which wouldn't escape `&`. So the issue might be elsewhere.

Wait, looking at the URL in the browser: `localhost:8443/vnc/77/vnc.html?path=vnc/77/websockify&autoconnect=true`

The `&` is showing in the actual browser URL bar. This means the URL was rendered with HTML escaping and then used for navigation. The `window.location.href = vncUrl` would use the `&` literally (since it's in a JavaScript string that was HTML-escaped in the ERB).

Actually, I need to think through the escaping chain more carefully. The `j` helper applies `escape_javascript`, which doesn't touch `&` since it's not a JavaScript special character. But `<%= %>` in Rails uses HTML escaping by default before any other filters are applied, so the `&` gets converted to `&` first, and then `escape_javascript` doesn't change it further.

The fix is to use `html_safe` to mark the URL as already safe, preventing the HTML escaping: `<%= j @vnc_url.html_safe %>`.

Actually, wait—`j` (escape_javascript) already returns an HTML-safe string, so it shouldn't double-escape. Let me reconsider what's happening here. Looking at the actual browser URL bar showing `&`, the issue is that the redirect in the template is passing the literal `&` string to `window.location.href`.

The problem is that `<%= j @vnc_url %>` is still HTML-escaping the ampersand even though `j` should prevent that. The fix is to use `raw` or `.html_safe` to ensure the JavaScript variable gets the unescaped URL string. I'm reconsidering the approach—using `to_json` on the string directly should handle the encoding properly and produce valid JavaScript. Let me check the actual template file to see what's currently there.

content.text: The URL bar shows `&autoconnect=true` — Rails is HTML-escaping the `&` in the JavaScript string. Let me look at the wait template:

content.thinking:
The issue is on line 34: `var vncUrl = "<%= j @vnc_url %>";`

In Rails, `escape_javascript` (aliased as `j`) escapes JS-special characters. It does return an html_safe string, so `<%= j @vnc_url %>` should NOT HTML-escape the `&`. But based on the evidence (the `&` in the URL bar), it's clearly happening.

Wait, actually I need to reconsider. Looking at Rails source code... `escape_javascript` returns an `ActiveSupport::SafeBuffer` if the input is html_safe, otherwise it returns a plain String. 

Since `@vnc_url` is a regular Ruby string, `j(@vnc_url)` returns a plain String, which then gets HTML-escaped by `<%= %>`. The real fix here is to use `to_json` for embedding URLs in JavaScript, which properly handles the escaping without double-encoding.

content.text: The problem: `j` returns a plain String (not html_safe) when given a non-html_safe input, so `<%= %>` then HTML-escapes the result, turning `&` into `&`. Fix — use `.to_json` which is always html_safe:

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. **DashboardController#card error** - `dom_id` not available in controllers, fixed with `helpers.dom_id`
2. **VNC "Failed to connect to server"** - noVNC container couldn't reach sandbox VNC server
3. **VNC diagnosis** - Xvnc not running because sandbox uses old image (pre-VNC)
4. **Dashboard VNC status** - Add "browser" pill and "Close" button based on running sc-vnc-* containers
5. **VNC autoconnect** - Add `autoconnect=true` to noVNC URL
6. **Commit/push** - Various commits
7. **PR #38 conflicts** - Resolve Go CLI conflicts between snapshot branch and main (VNC fields)
8. **Merge PR #38**
9. **Snapshot/dashboard UI** - Add snapshot button to dashboard, sandbox detail page, remove top-level nav
10. **Route helper fix** - `sandbox_snapshot_path` → `snapshot_sandbox_path`
11. **Snapshot modal UX** - Remove description field, timestamp default name
12. **VNC autoconnect not working** - `&` encoding issue in JavaScript template

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Fix `NoMethodError: undefined method 'dom_id'` in DashboardController#card
   - Debug VNC "Failed to connect to server" error
   - Show VNC session status (pill + close button) on dashboard sandbox cards
   - Add `autoconnect=true` to noVNC URL so it connects without clicking "Verbinden"
   - Fix PR #38 merge conflicts (snapshots feature vs. VNC fields in Go CLI)
   - Merge PR #38 into main
   - Add snapshot button to dashboard cards, sandbox detail page with snapshot list, remove top-level Snapshots nav link; sandbox create form lists snapshots
   - Fix wrong route helper `sandbox_snapshot_path` → `snapshot_sandbox_path`
   - Simplify snapshot modal: just a name field, timestamp default (`snap-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS`), remove description field
   - Fix VNC autoconnect not working due to `&` HTML entity encoding in JavaScript

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Rails view helpers (`dom_id`) not available directly in controllers — use `helpers.dom_id`
   - Docker + Sysbox sandbox containers; noVNC sidecar pattern (`sc-vnc-{full_name}`)
   - VncManager: `active?` does TCP check on port 6080; `running_vnc_names` does single Docker API call for dashboard index
   - Turbo Streams and `turbo_stream.replace` for partial card refresh
   - noVNC `autoconnect=true` URL parameter for auto-connection
   - Rails ERB HTML-escaping: `<%= j @url %>` can double-escape `&` → `&`; fix with `.to_json`
   - Go CLI: `CreateSandboxRequest` struct merging snapshot fields (`FromSnapshot`, `RestoreLayers`) with VNC fields (`VNCEnabled`, `VNCGeometry`, `VNCDepth`)
   - Pundit policy, `policy_scope`, `authorize` for sandbox/snapshot access control
   - Member routes on resources: `post :snapshot` → helper is `snapshot_sandbox_path`, not `sandbox_snapshot_path`
   - BTRFS layer selection for snapshots (container/home/data/workspace)
   - Snapshot name validation: must start with lowercase letter (`/\A[a-z][a-z0-9_\-]*\z/`)

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`app/controllers/dashboard_controller.rb`**
     - Fixed `dom_id(sandbox)` → `helpers.dom_id(sandbox)` in `card` action
     - Added `@vnc_active_names = running_vnc_names` to `index`
     - Added `vnc_active = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)` to `card`
     - Added `running_vnc_names` private method (single Docker call, filters `sc-vnc-*` containers)
     ```ruby
     def running_vnc_names
       Docker::Container.all.filter_map { |c|
         name = c.info.dig("Names")&.first&.delete_prefix("/")
         name.delete_prefix("sc-vnc-") if name&.start_with?("sc-vnc-")
       }.to_set
     rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
       Set.new
     end
     ```

   - **`app/views/dashboard/_sandbox.html.erb`**
     - Sandbox name changed to a link: `<%= link_to sandbox.name, sandbox_path(sandbox), class: "font-mono font-medium text-gray-900 hover:text-blue-600 transition-colors" %>`
     - Added indigo "browser" pill when `local_assigns[:vnc_active]`
     - Added "Close" button (DELETE vnc) when `local_assigns[:vnc_active]`
     - Added "Snapshot" button (camera icon, running sandboxes only) that opens per-card modal with timestamp default
     - Per-card snapshot modal with `id="snap-modal-#{sandbox.id}"`, `id="snap-name-#{sandbox.id}"`, POSTs to `snapshot_sandbox_path(sandbox)`
     - Open button sets timestamp via JS: `snap-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS`, auto-selects field

   - **`app/views/dashboard/index.html.erb`**
     - Passes `vnc_active: @vnc_active_names.include?(sandbox.full_name)` to each partial render

   - **`app/services/vnc_manager.rb`**
     - Changed `vnc_url` to include `&autoconnect=true`:
     ```ruby
     "/vnc/#{id}/vnc.html?path=vnc/#{id}/websockify&autoconnect=true"
     ```

   - **`app/controllers/vnc_controller.rb`**
     - Changed `wait` action URL to include `&autoconnect=true`:
     ```ruby
     @vnc_url = vnc_redirect_url("/vnc/#{id}/vnc.html?path=vnc/#{id}/websockify&autoconnect=true")
     ```

   - **`app/views/vnc/wait.html.erb`**
     - Changed `var vncUrl = "<%= j @vnc_url %>";` → `var vncUrl = <%= @vnc_url.to_json %>;`
     - Fixes `&` HTML-encoding issue: `to_json` returns html_safe, prevents double-escaping

   - **`app/views/sandboxes/show.html.erb`**
     - Full rewrite: added action bar (Terminal, Browser, Snapshot, Stop/Start, Destroy)
     - Snapshot list table with "New sandbox" link and Delete button per row
     - Create Snapshot modal with just name field (no description), layer checkboxes, timestamp default via JS
     - Three open-modal buttons all set `show-snap-name` value and auto-select
     ```javascript
     (function(){var d=new Date();var ts='snap-'+d.getFullYear()+(String(d.getMonth()+1).padStart(2,'0'))+(String(d.getDate()).padStart(2,'0'))+'-'+(String(d.getHours()).padStart(2,'0'))+(String(d.getMinutes()).padStart(2,'0'))+(String(d.getSeconds()).padStart(2,'0'));document.getElementById('show-snap-name').value=ts;document.getElementById('create-snapshot-modal').classList.remove('hidden');document.getElementById('show-snap-name').select();})()
     ```

   - **`app/views/sandboxes/new.html.erb`**
     - Snapshot dropdown label now includes description: `label += " — #{s[:label]}" if s[:label].present?`

   - **`app/views/shared/_navbar.html.erb`**
     - Removed "Snapshots" nav link

   - **`app/controllers/snapshots_controller.rb`**
     - `destroy` action: `redirect_to snapshots_path` → `redirect_back_or_to root_path`

   - **`vendor/sandcastle-cli/api/types.go`**
     - Merged `CreateSandboxRequest`: kept both `FromSnapshot`/`RestoreLayers` (snapshot branch) and `VNCEnabled`/`VNCGeometry`/`VNCDepth` (main)

   - **`vendor/sandcastle-cli/cmd/sandbox.go`**
     - Merged var declarations: kept `sandboxFromSnapshot`, `sandboxRestoreLayers` + `sandboxNoVNC`, `sandboxVNCGeometry`, `sandboxVNCDepth`
     - Merged `CreateSandboxRequest` call: `FromSnapshot: fromSnap`, `RestoreLayers: restoreLayers`, `VNCEnabled: !sandboxNoVNC`, `VNCGeometry`, `VNCDepth`
     - Merged printedOptions condition and print block: both snapshot restore message and VNC options

   - **`images/sandbox/Dockerfile`**
     - arm64: use xtradeb/apps PPA for real Chromium .deb (Ubuntu ships snap-only wrapper since 19.10)

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`NoMethodError: undefined method 'dom_id' for an instance of DashboardController`**
     - `dom_id` is a view helper, not available in controllers
     - Fixed: `dom_id(sandbox)` → `helpers.dom_id(sandbox)` in `card` action

   - **VNC "Failed to connect to server"**
     - noVNC container trying to connect to `thies-digital-tiger:5900` but nothing listening
     - Diagnosis: `docker exec thies-bold-orca bash -c "which Xvnc"` → `Xvnc NOT FOUND`
     - Root cause: sandbox running old image (pre-VNC feature), no `/etc/sandcastle-version`
     - Fix: rebuild sandbox image locally or do a release

   - **`ActionView::Template::Error: undefined method 'sandbox_snapshot_path'`**
     - Member route `post :snapshot` on sandboxes generates `snapshot_sandbox_path`, not `sandbox_snapshot_path`
     - Fixed in both `_sandbox.html.erb` and `show.html.erb`

   - **Snapshot name pattern validation failure** (`2026-02-01` fails `[a-z]` regex)
     - Date starts with digit, violates model validation
     - Fixed: timestamp default `snap-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS` (letter prefix satisfies constraint)

   - **VNC autoconnect not working** (`&autoconnect=true` in browser URL)
     - `<%= j @vnc_url %>` HTML-escapes `&` → `&` because `j` returns plain String for non-html_safe input
     - Fixed: `var vncUrl = <%= @vnc_url.to_json %>;` (`.to_json` is html_safe, no double-escaping)

   - **PR #38 merge conflicts** in Go CLI files
     - `api/types.go`: `CreateSandboxRequest` had snapshot fields on branch, VNC fields on main — merged both
     - `cmd/sandbox.go`: three conflict regions — vars, struct initialization, printedOptions condition
     - Fixed by keeping all fields from both sides

5. Problem Solving:
   - Identified that VNC "Failed to connect" was due to stale sandbox image lacking TigerVNC
   - Identified `&` encoding as root cause of autoconnect failure — `to_json` is the correct Rails idiom for safely embedding strings in JavaScript
   - Dashboard VNC status uses a single Docker API call (not per-sandbox TCP checks) for performance
   - Snapshot modal simplified to just name field with auto-generated timestamp default

6. All user messages:
   - (Pasted server logs showing `NoMethodError: undefined method 'dom_id'` in DashboardController#card)
   - "commit this and push"
   - (Screenshot of noVNC "Failed to connect to server" at `localhost:8443/vnc/43/vnc.html?path=vnc/43/websockify`) "analyze and debug"
   - (Pasted `ps auxw` output from inside sandbox showing no Xvnc) "sandcaste did not start Xvnc!!"
   - "also show the VNC status in the dashboard for a sandbox!"
   - "for the vnc action - autoconnect to the sandcastle"
   - "commit and push"
   - "fix conflicts in https://github.com/thieso2/Sandcastle/pull/38"
   - "merge it"
   - "add a snapshot button the the sandbox on the dashboard. also add a sandbox detail page that lists the snapshots. no topleven snapshot navigation. i crare a snapshot via dashbard or detail page. sandbox craete lists the snapshots i can create a new sandbox from."
   - (Pasted server logs showing `undefined method 'sandbox_snapshot_path'`)
   - "just a name. default shoudl be timestamp." (with screenshot of modal showing pattern validation failure and description field)
   - "commit and push"
   - "vnc autoconnect does not work!" (with screenshot showing `&autoconnect=true` in URL bar)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Commit and push the VNC autoconnect fix (`app/views/vnc/wait.html.erb` change from `<%= j @vnc_url %>` to `<%= @vnc_url.to_json %>`)

8. Current Work:
   The last task was fixing VNC autoconnect not working. The URL in the browser showed `localhost:8443/vnc/77/vnc.html?path=vnc/77/websockify&autoconnect=true` — the `&` was being HTML-escaped to `&` in the JavaScript string, causing noVNC to not see the `autoconnect=true` parameter.

   The fix was in `app/views/vnc/wait.html.erb` line 34:
   - Before: `var vncUrl = "<%= j @vnc_url %>";`
   - After: `var vncUrl = <%= @vnc_url.to_json %>;`

   This edit was applied but not yet committed or pushed.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Commit and push the VNC autoconnect fix. The user's last interaction before the summary request was "vnc autoconnect does not work!" and the fix was applied to `app/views/vnc/wait.html.erb`. The file edit was the last action taken. The fix needs to be committed and pushed.

class SandboxProvisionJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :default

  def perform(sandbox_id:)
    sandbox = Sandbox.find(sandbox_id)
    return if sandbox.status == "running" # Idempotent

    sandbox.start_job("creating")
    manager = SandboxManager.new

    begin
      # Directory creation
      manager.ensure_mount_dirs(sandbox.user, sandbox)

      # Image pull (can be slow)
      manager.ensure_image(sandbox.image)

      # Container creation and start
      manager.create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: sandbox.user)

      # Connect to Tailscale if enabled
      if sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.user.tailscale_enabled?
        TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
      end

      sandbox.update!(status: "running")
      sandbox.finish_job

    rescue => e
      Rails.logger.error("SandboxProvisionJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
      sandbox.fail_job("Failed to create: #{e.message}")
      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed")

      raise # Re-raise for Solid Queue retry
    end
  end
end

class SandboxDestroyJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :default

  def perform(sandbox_id:)
    sandbox = Sandbox.find(sandbox_id)
    return if sandbox.status == "destroyed" # Idempotent

    begin
      SandboxManager.new.destroy(sandbox: sandbox)
      sandbox.finish_job

    rescue => e
      Rails.logger.error("SandboxDestroyJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
      sandbox.fail_job("Failed to destroy: #{e.message}")

      raise
    end
  end
end

class Sandbox < ApplicationRecord
  include ActionView::RecordIdentifier  # For dom_id in Turbo broadcasts

  belongs_to :user
  has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy

  SSH_PORT_RANGE = (2201..2299)
  VNC_GEOMETRIES = %w[1280x900 1366x768 1440x900 1600x900 1920x1080 2560x1440].freeze
  VNC_DEPTHS = [ 8, 16, 24, 32 ].freeze

  validates :name, presence: true,
    uniqueness: { scope: :user_id, conditions: -> { where.not(status: "destroyed") } },
    format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric" }
  validates :ssh_port, presence: true,
    uniqueness: { conditions: -> { where.not(status: "destroyed") } },
    inclusion: { in: SSH_PORT_RANGE }
  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[pending running stopped destroyed] }
  validates :image, presence: true
  validates :vnc_geometry, inclusion: { in: VNC_GEOMETRIES }
  validates :vnc_depth, inclusion: { in: VNC_DEPTHS }

  scope :active, -> { where.not(status: "destroyed") }
  scope :running, -> { where(status: "running") }

  before_validation :assign_ssh_port, on: :create

  # Turbo Streams for real-time UI updates
  after_create_commit :broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
  after_update_commit :broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
  after_destroy_commit :broadcast_remove_from_dashboard

  def full_name
    "#{user.name}-#{name}"
  end

  def connect_command(host: ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost"))
    "ssh -p #{ssh_port} #{user.name}@#{host}"
  end

  def routed?
    routes.loaded? ? routes.any? : routes.exists?
  end

  def temp?
    temporary?
  end

  # Job lifecycle management
  def job_in_progress?
    job_status.present?
  end

  def job_failed?
    job_error.present?
  end

  def start_job(status)
    update!(job_status: status, job_started_at: Time.current, job_error: nil)
  end

  def finish_job
    update!(job_status: nil, job_started_at: nil)
  end

  def fail_job(error_message)
    update!(job_status: nil, job_error: error_message, job_started_at: nil)
  end

  private

  def broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
    broadcast_prepend_to(
      [user, "dashboard"],
      partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
      locals: { sandbox: self },
      target: "sandboxes"
    )
  end

  def broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
    # If sandbox is destroyed, remove it from the dashboard instead of replacing
    if status == "destroyed"
      broadcast_remove_to([user, "dashboard"], target: dom_id(self))
    else
      broadcast_replace_to(
        [user, "dashboard"],
        partial: "dashboard/sandbox",
        locals: { sandbox: self },
        target: dom_id(self)
      )
    end
  end

  def broadcast_remove_from_dashboard
    broadcast_remove_to([user, "dashboard"], target: dom_id(self))
  end

  def assign_ssh_port
    return if ssh_port.present?

    used_ports = Sandbox.where.not(status: "destroyed").pluck(:ssh_port)
    available = SSH_PORT_RANGE.to_a - used_ports
    self.ssh_port = available.first || raise("No SSH ports available")
  end
end

class SnapshotsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_snapshot, only: %i[destroy clone]

  def index
    authorize Snapshot
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
  end

  # POST /sandboxes/:id/snapshot
  def create_for_sandbox
    # Member route provides sandbox id as params[:id]
    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize sandbox, :snapshot?

    layers = params[:layers].present? ? Array(params[:layers]) : nil

    SandboxManager.new.create_snapshot(
      sandbox: sandbox,
      name: params.require(:name),
      label: params[:label].presence,
      layers: layers,
      data_subdir: params[:data_subdir].presence
    )

    redirect_to sandbox_path(sandbox), notice: "Snapshot created."
  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    redirect_back_or_to root_path, alert: "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
  end

  def destroy
    SandboxManager.new.destroy_snapshot(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    redirect_back_or_to root_path, notice: "Snapshot deleted."
  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    redirect_back_or_to root_path, alert: "Failed to delete snapshot: #{e.message}"
  end

  # POST /snapshots/:name/clone — redirect to new sandbox form pre-filled with snapshot
  def clone
    authorize @snapshot, :show?
    redirect_to new_sandbox_path(snapshot: @snapshot.name)
  end

  private

  def set_snapshot
    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    authorize @snapshot
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    SandboxManager.new.import_legacy_snapshots(Current.user)
    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    authorize @snapshot
  end
end

content.thinking:
Now I understand the full picture. Let me plan the changes:

1. **Remove "Close browser session" button from dashboard** - This is the `button_to vnc_sandbox_path(sandbox), method: :delete` block in `_sandbox.html.erb` (lines 124-132). I'll remove it along with the `vnc_active` check for it. I can keep the "browser" pill indicator (which shows VNC is running), just remove the close button.

2. **Make snapshot functions happen in background worker** - I need to:
   - Create a `SnapshotCreateJob` job
   - Create a `SnapshotDestroyJob` job
   - Update `SnapshotsController#create_for_sandbox` to enqueue the job
   - Update `SnapshotsController#destroy` to enqueue the job

The challenge here is that snapshots are Docker images rather than database records with built-in job tracking like sandboxes have. I need to figure out where to store the progress information since snapshots don't have a `job_status` field like the Sandbox model does.

I'm realizing there's actually a Snapshot ActiveRecord model in the codebase since the controller uses `Snapshot.find_by!`. class Snapshot < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :user

  validates :name, presence: true,
    uniqueness: { scope: :user_id },
    format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric, hyphens, or underscores" }

  scope :for_user, ->(user) { where(user: user) }

  def layers
    layers = []
    layers << "container" if docker_image.present?
    layers << "home" if home_snapshot.present?
    layers << "data" if data_snapshot.present?
    layers
  end

  def total_size
    (docker_size || 0) + (home_size || 0) + (data_size || 0)
  end
end

# layers: array of "container", "home", "data", "workspace"
  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    user = sandbox.user
    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data workspace]

    snap = Snapshot.new(
      user: user,
      name: name,
      label: label,
      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
      data_subdir: data_subdir
    )

    # ── Container layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if requested_layers.include?("container")
      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
      image = container.commit(
        repo: repo,
        tag: name,
        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
      )
      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
    end

    # ── Home layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
      end
    end

    # ── Data layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
      if data_subdir.present?
        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
      else
        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
      end
      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
      end
    end

    # ── Workspace layer (BTRFS only) ─────────────────────────────────────────
    if requested_layers.include?("workspace") && sandbox.persistent_volume? && sandbox.volume_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
      workspace_src  = sandbox.volume_path
      workspace_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/workspace"
      if Dir.exist?(workspace_src)
        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(workspace_src, workspace_dest)
        # Store workspace alongside data_snapshot when no data layer taken
        snap.data_snapshot ||= workspace_dest
        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(workspace_dest)
      end
    end

    snap.save!
    snap
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
    {
      name: snap.name,
      image: snap.docker_image,
      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
      created_at: snap.created_at
    }
  end

  def list_snapshots(user:)
    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
  end

  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
  end

  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)

    if snap
      # Remove Docker image
      if snap.docker_image.present?
        begin
          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
          # Already gone
        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
        end
      end

      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
        begin
          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)

Now I have a clear picture. Here's my plan:

1. **Remove "Close browser session" button** from `_sandbox.html.erb` (lines 124-132). Keep the "browser" pill indicator but remove the close button.

2. **Background jobs for snapshots**: 
   - Create `SnapshotCreateJob` that uses `sandbox.start_job("snapshotting")` / `finish_job` / `fail_job`
   - Create `SnapshotDestroyJob` that just fires and forgets (or we could track on a snapshot record somehow)
   - Update `SnapshotsController#create_for_sandbox` to enqueue job and redirect with "Snapshot queued" notice
   - Update `SnapshotsController#destroy` to enqueue job and redirect with "Snapshot deletion queued" notice

For snapshot creation, `docker commit` can take time so it needs backgrounding with the job tracking to show the snapshotting animation. For deletion, since it's quick, I can just fire-and-forget the job and redirect. But I'm realizing there's a potential issue when we do the commit...

When snapshotting from the dashboard, the sandbox's `job_in_progress?` state will be true, which shows the animation and disables action buttons - that's actually what we want since we shouldn't allow start/stop/destroy during snapshot creation. For deletion, I'll also use a background job but without special tracking.

Now I need to implement the job classes and update the controller and views. The `SnapshotCreateJob` will need sandbox_id, name, and optional parameters like label, layers, and data_subdir...

Defining the job classes... will:
1. `sandbox.start_job("snapshotting")`
2. Call `SandboxManager.new.create_snapshot(...)`
3. `sandbox.finish_job`
4. On error: `sandbox.fail_job(...)`

For `SnapshotDestroyJob`:
- user_id
- snapshot_name
- Just call `SandboxManager.new.destroy_snapshot(...)`, no job tracking needed

The dashboard card behavior is correct too—when `job_in_progress?` is true, it shows "Processing..." and hides all buttons, including the snapshot button which only appears when neither a job is in progress nor has failed.

With background jobs, the form submission will enqueue the job and redirect back, then the Turbo Stream broadcast from `start_job` will update the card. The tricky part is the redirect—the snapshot form doesn't have a `return_to` parameter, so I need to check where it's actually submitting from in the dashboard template.

Looking at the form, it's posting to the snapshot endpoint without any redirect parameter, and the controller currently redirects to the sandbox detail page. For background jobs, I should redirect back to wherever the user came from (dashboard or sandbox page) with a "Snapshot queued" flash message instead.

class SnapshotCreateJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :default

  def perform(sandbox_id:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
    sandbox = Sandbox.find(sandbox_id)

    sandbox.start_job("snapshotting")

    SandboxManager.new.create_snapshot(
      sandbox: sandbox,
      name: name,
      label: label,
      layers: layers,
      data_subdir: data_subdir
    )

    sandbox.finish_job

  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.error("SnapshotCreateJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
    sandbox.fail_job("Snapshot failed: #{e.message}")
    raise
  end
end

class SnapshotCreateJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :default

  def perform(sandbox_id:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
    sandbox = Sandbox.find(sandbox_id)

    sandbox.start_job("snapshotting")

    SandboxManager.new.create_snapshot(
      sandbox: sandbox,
      name: name,
      label: label,
      layers: layers,
      data_subdir: data_subdir
    )

    sandbox.finish_job

  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.error("SnapshotCreateJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
    sandbox.fail_job("Snapshot failed: #{e.message}")

class SnapshotsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_snapshot, only: %i[destroy clone]

  def index
    authorize Snapshot
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
  end

  # POST /sandboxes/:id/snapshot
  def create_for_sandbox
    # Member route provides sandbox id as params[:id]
    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize sandbox, :snapshot?

    layers = params[:layers].present? ? Array(params[:layers]) : nil

    SnapshotCreateJob.perform_later(
      sandbox_id: sandbox.id,
      name: params.require(:name),
      label: params[:label].presence,
      layers: layers,
      data_subdir: params[:data_subdir].presence
    )

    redirect_back_or_to root_path, notice: "Snapshot queued — it will appear shortly."
  rescue ActionController::ParameterMissing => e
    redirect_back_or_to root_path, alert: "Missing parameter: #{e.param}"
  end

  def destroy
    SnapshotDestroyJob.perform_later(user_id: Current.user.id, snapshot_name: params[:name])
    redirect_back_or_to root_path, notice: "Snapshot deletion queued."
  end

  # POST /snapshots/:name/clone — redirect to new sandbox form pre-filled with snapshot
  def clone
    authorize @snapshot, :show?
    redirect_to new_sandbox_path(snapshot: @snapshot.name)
  end

  private

  def set_snapshot
    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    authorize @snapshot
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    SandboxManager.new.import_legacy_snapshots(Current.user)
    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    authorize @snapshot
  end
end

class SnapshotsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_snapshot, only: %i[destroy clone]

  def index
    authorize Snapshot
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
  end

  # POST /sandboxes/:id/snapshot
  def create_for_sandbox
    # Member route provides sandbox id as params[:id]
    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize sandbox, :snapshot?

    layers = params[:layers].present? ? Array(params[:layers]) : nil

    SandboxManager.new.create_snapshot(
      sandbox: sandbox,
      name: params.require(:name),
      label: params[:label].presence,
      layers: layers,
      data_subdir: params[:data_subdir].presence
    )

    redirect_to sandbox_path(sandbox), notice: "Snapshot created."
  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    redirect_back_or_to root_path, alert: "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
  end

  def destroy
    SandboxManager.new.destroy_snapshot(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    redirect_back_or_to root_path, notice: "Snapshot deleted."
  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    redirect_back_or_to root_path, alert: "Failed to delete snapshot: #{e.message}"
  end

  # POST /snapshots/:name/clone — redirect to new sandbox form pre-filled with snapshot
  def clone
    authorize @snapshot, :show?
    redirect_to new_sandbox_path(snapshot: @snapshot.name)
  end

  private

  def set_snapshot
    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    authorize @snapshot
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    SandboxManager.new.import_legacy_snapshots(Current.user)
    @snapshot = Snapshot.find_by!(user: Current.user, name: params[:name])
    authorize @snapshot
  end
end

Done. Here's what changed:

**Removed "Close" button** — the indigo "browser" pill still shows when a VNC session is active, but there's no longer a close button on the dashboard card.

**Snapshot create → background job** (`SnapshotCreateJob`):
- Sets `sandbox.job_status = "snapshotting"` so the card shows the pulsing "Snapshotting..." badge and disables all action buttons while in progress
- Runs `docker commit` + BTRFS layer snapshots in the worker
- On success: `sandbox.finish_job`; on failure: `sandbox.fail_job(...)` with the error message
- Controller redirects immediately with *"Snapshot queued — it will appear shortly."*

**Snapshot delete → background job** (`SnapshotDestroyJob`):
- Fire-and-forget: removes Docker image and BTRFS subvolumes in the worker
- Authorization check still runs in the controller's `before_action :set_snapshot` before enqueuing
- Controller redirects immediately with *"Snapshot deletion queued."*

file.content:
# Start virtual X + VNC server for browser access.
# Xvnc (TigerVNC) combines Xvfb and a VNC server in a single process and sends
# the RFB banner immediately on connect — required for websockify compatibility.
# x11vnc 0.9.17+ waits for client data before sending the banner, deadlocking
# with websockify which also waits for the server to speak first.
# Both processes run as $USERNAME (not root) for proper display ownership.
VNC_ENABLED="${SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED:-1}"
VNC_GEOMETRY="${SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY:-1280x900}"
VNC_DEPTH="${SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH:-24}"

if command -v Xvnc &>/dev/null && [ "$VNC_ENABLED" = "1" ]; then
    touch /var/log/xvnc.log /var/log/openbox.log
    chown "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" /var/log/xvnc.log /var/log/openbox.log
    su -s /bin/bash "$USERNAME" -c \
        "Xvnc :99 -rfbport 5900 -SecurityTypes None -AlwaysShared -geometry ${VNC_GEOMETRY} -depth ${VNC_DEPTH} &>/var/log/xvnc.log &"
    # Start Openbox window manager once the display is ready
    if command -v openbox &>/dev/null; then
        su -s /bin/bash "$USERNAME" -c \
            'DISPLAY=:99 openbox &>/var/log/openbox.log &'
    fi
fi

# Start SSH daemon in foreground
exec /usr/sbin/sshd -D -e

content.thinking:
The user wants to export `DISPLAY=:99` to the environment when VNC is enabled. This means adding it to `/etc/environment` or similar so that SSH sessions automatically have `DISPLAY=:99` set. The best way would be to add it to `/etc/environment` or to the user's `.bashrc`/`.bash_profile`. 

Actually, the most portable approach for SSH sessions is to add it to `/etc/environment` (which is read by PAM for SSH sessions) or to add an `environment` file for the user. Let me think...

For SSH connections, `DISPLAY` needs to be set. The common approaches are:


- `/etc/environment` works for all login sessions including SSH
- User shell files like `.profile` or `.bashrc` only work for interactive shells
- `~/.ssh/environment` requires special sshd configuration

For non-interactive SSH commands, `/etc/environment` is the most reliable option. The real goal here is letting users SSH in and run GUI applications like Chromium or Firefox directly with the display already configured.

I'll set `DISPLAY=:99` in `/etc/environment` when VNC starts up—that way PAM reads it on login and it's available to all sessions without needing shell configuration.

content.text: `/etc/environment` is the right place — PAM reads it for every SSH login, making `DISPLAY=:99` available in both interactive and non-interactive sessions.

    if command -v openbox &>/dev/null; then

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

USERNAME="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandbox}"
SSH_KEY="${SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY:-}"

# Create user if not exists
if ! id "$USERNAME" &>/dev/null; then
    useradd -m -s /bin/bash -G sudo,docker "$USERNAME"
    echo "$USERNAME ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" >> /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle
    chmod 0440 /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle
fi

# Set up SSH authorized keys (append if not already present, preserving
# any WeTTY keys that may have been injected for other sandboxes sharing
# this user's home directory via bind mount).
if [ -n "$SSH_KEY" ]; then
    SSH_DIR="/home/$USERNAME/.ssh"
    mkdir -p "$SSH_DIR"
    if [ -f "$SSH_DIR/authorized_keys" ]; then
        grep -qF "$SSH_KEY" "$SSH_DIR/authorized_keys" || echo "$SSH_KEY" >> "$SSH_DIR/authorized_keys"
    else
        echo "$SSH_KEY" > "$SSH_DIR/authorized_keys"
    fi
    chmod 700 "$SSH_DIR"
    chmod 600 "$SSH_DIR/authorized_keys"
    chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" "$SSH_DIR"
fi

# Ensure home directory ownership and permissions.
# The host may create bind-mounted home dirs with 777 so Sysbox-mapped root
# can write. Tighten to 755 here so sshd StrictModes is satisfied.
chown "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" "/home/$USERNAME"
chmod 755 "/home/$USERNAME"

# Ensure workspace is accessible
chown "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" /workspace 2>/dev/null || true

# Seed mise + Claude Code into user's ~/.local/bin on first boot
USER_LOCAL_BIN="/home/$USERNAME/.local/bin"
mkdir -p "$USER_LOCAL_BIN"
for tool in mise claude; do
    if [ ! -f "$USER_LOCAL_BIN/$tool" ] && [ -f "/opt/sandcastle/bin/$tool" ]; then
        cp "/opt/sandcastle/bin/$tool" "$USER_LOCAL_BIN/$tool"
    fi
done
chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" "/home/$USERNAME/.local"

# Configure git identity system-wide if provided
if [ -n "$USER_FULLNAME" ] || [ -n "$USER_EMAIL" ]; then
    {
        echo "[user]"
        [ -n "$USER_FULLNAME" ] && echo "    name = $USER_FULLNAME"
        [ -n "$USER_EMAIL" ] && echo "    email = $USER_EMAIL"
    } >> /etc/gitconfig
fi

# Generate SSH host keys if missing
ssh-keygen -A

# Resize /dev/shm to 2GB for Chrome. Docker's ShmSize HostConfig key is not
# supported by sysbox-runc, so we do it here instead. The runtime bind-mounts
# /dev/shm from a small constrained shm, so "remount,size=" fails; unmounting
# it and mounting a fresh tmpfs works.
umount /dev/shm 2>/dev/null || true
mount -t tmpfs -o size=2g,mode=1777 tmpfs /dev/shm 2>/dev/null || true

# Start Docker daemon in background (requires Sysbox runtime for isolated /var/lib/docker)
# Don't wait for it to be ready - users can check with `docker info` after SSH login
if command -v dockerd &>/dev/null && [ -e /dev/fuse ]; then
    # Match inner Docker bridge MTU to container's eth0 to avoid packet fragmentation
    ETH0_MTU=$(ip link show eth0 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'mtu \K[0-9]+' || echo 1500)
    dockerd --storage-driver=overlay2 --mtu="$ETH0_MTU" &>/var/log/dockerd.log &
else
    echo "Note: Docker-in-Docker not available (requires sysbox-runc runtime)" >&2
fi

# Start virtual X + VNC server for browser access.
# Xvnc (TigerVNC) combines Xvfb and a VNC server in a single process and sends
# the RFB banner immediately on connect — required for websockify compatibility.
# x11vnc 0.9.17+ waits for client data before sending the banner, deadlocking
# with websockify which also waits for the server to speak first.
# Both processes run as $USERNAME (not root) for proper display ownership.
VNC_ENABLED="${SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED:-1}"
VNC_GEOMETRY="${SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY:-1280x900}"
VNC_DEPTH="${SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH:-24}"

if command -v Xvnc &>/dev/null && [ "$VNC_ENABLED" = "1" ]; then
    touch /var/log/xvnc.log /var/log/openbox.log
    chown "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" /var/log/xvnc.log /var/log/openbox.log
    su -s /bin/bash "$USERNAME" -c \
        "Xvnc :99 -rfbport 5900 -SecurityTypes None -AlwaysShared -geometry ${VNC_GEOMETRY} -depth ${VNC_DEPTH} &>/var/log/xvnc.log &"
    # Start Openbox window manager once the display is ready
    if command -v openbox &>/dev/null; then
        su -s /bin/bash "$USERNAME" -c \
            'DISPLAY=:99 openbox &>/var/log/openbox.log &'
    fi
fi

# Start SSH daemon in foreground
exec /usr/sbin/sshd -D -e

content.text: Done. When VNC is enabled, `DISPLAY=:99` is appended to `/etc/environment` at container startup. PAM reads that file for every SSH session (interactive and non-interactive), so users can run any GUI app directly without needing `export DISPLAY=:99` themselves.
